translations.literal: " Du Yu commentary Zuo Tradition says: "Square zhang makes du—three du make zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu defines wall units: one square zhang per du, three du per zhi.

sentences.zh: 」字林曰：「呀，大空也。

translations.literal: " Character Forest says: "Ya—great hollow.
translations.idiomatic: The Character Forest glosses ya as "wide hollow" (of moats).

sentences.zh: 」音火加反。

translations.literal: " Sound huo-jia fan.
translations.idiomatic: Pronounced like huo plus jia reversed.

sentences.zh: 周禮：「國方九里，旁三門。

translations.literal: Zhou Rites: "State square nine li—side three gates.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou Rites prescribes nine-li square capitals with three gates per side.

sentences.zh: 」每門有大路，故曰三條。

translations.literal: " Each gate has great road—therefore called three strips.
translations.idiomatic: Each gate fronts a major avenue—the "three strips."

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注周禮云「天子城十二門，通十二子」也。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan commentary Zhou Rites says "Son of Heaven city twelve gates—penetrate twelve zi" also.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan notes twelve gates matching the twelve earthly branches.

sentences.zh: 注[一三]字林曰：「閭，里門也。

translations.literal: Commentary [thirteen]: Character Forest says: "Lü—ward gate.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The Character Forest defines lü as a ward gate.

sentences.zh: 閻，裡中門也。

translations.literal: Yan—lane middle gate.
translations.idiomatic: Yan is the inner gate of a ward.

sentences.zh: 」且千，言多也。

translations.literal: " Moreover thousand—speaks many.
translations.idiomatic: "Nearly a thousand" stresses sheer number.

sentences.zh: 漢宮閣疏曰：「長安九市，其六在道西，三在道東。

translations.literal: Han palace pavilion digest says: "Chang'an nine markets—six on road west—three on road east.
translations.idiomatic: The Han palace gazetteer lists nine markets—six west of the avenue, three east.

sentences.zh: 」隧，列肆道也。

translations.literal: " Sui—row shop lane.
translations.idiomatic: Sui means aisles between market stalls.

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注禮記曰：「廛，市物邸捨也。」

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan commentary Record of Rites says: "Chan—market goods lodging shed."
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan defines chan as shop stalls in the market.

sentences.zh: 注[一四]論語：「子適衛，冉有僕。

translations.literal: Commentary [fourteen]: Analects: "Master went to Wei—Ran You drove.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The Analects describes Confucius traveling to Wei with Ran You as driver.

sentences.zh: 子曰：『庶矣哉！

translations.literal: Master said: 'Numerous indeed!
translations.idiomatic: The Master exclaimed how populous it was.

sentences.zh: 』冉有曰：『既庶矣，又何加焉？

translations.literal: ' Ran You said: 'Already numerous—what further add?
translations.idiomatic: Ran You asked what more to do once the people multiplied.

sentences.zh: 』曰：『富之。

translations.literal: ' Said: 'Enrich them.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius answered: "Enrich them."

sentences.zh: 』」詩周頌云：「惠我無疆。

translations.literal: '" Poetry Zhou hymn says: "Benefit us without border.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou hymn prays for boundless blessing.

sentences.zh: 」疆，境也。

translations.literal: " Jiang—border.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang means "border" or "limit."

sentences.zh: 詩小雅曰：「彼都人士。

translations.literal: Poetry lesser ode says: "Those capital gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: The lesser ode praises capital gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 」毛萇注云：「城郭之域曰都。

translations.literal: " Mao Chang commentary says: "City wall's region called capital.
translations.idiomatic: Mao Chang defines "capital" as the walled precinct.

sentences.zh: 」五方謂四方及中央也。

translations.literal: " Five directions mean four directions and central.
translations.idiomatic: The five regions are the four quarters plus the center.

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「秦地五方雜錯。

translations.literal: Former book says: "Qin territory five directions mixed interwoven.
translations.idiomatic: The History of Western Han calls Qin a mosaic of peoples from every quarter.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注周禮曰：「肆，市中陳物處也。

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary Zhou Rites says: "Si—market middle display goods place.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan defines si as market stalls displaying goods.

sentences.zh: 」杜元凱注左傳云「姬﹑姜大國之女」也。

translations.literal: " Du Yuankai commentary Zuo Tradition says "Ji-Jiang great state's daughters" also.
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu glosses Ji and Jiang as daughters of great houses.

sentences.zh: 注[一五]豪俊遊俠謂朱家﹑郭解﹑原涉之類也。

translations.literal: Commentary [fifteen]: Heroic outstanding roaming bravoes mean Zhu Jia—Guo Jie—Yuan She's sorts.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Bold swordsmen include Zhu Jia, Guo Jie, Yuan She, and their ilk.

sentences.zh: 原﹑嘗*[謂]*平原君趙勝﹑孟嘗君田文也，春、陵謂春申君黃歇﹑信陵君無忌也，並招致賓客，名高天下也。

translations.literal: Yuan—Chang *[means]* Lord Pingyuan Zhao Sheng—Lord Mengchang Tian Wen—Chun—Ling mean Lord Chunshen Huang Xie—Lord Xinling Wu Ji—together summon retainers—name high realm.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan and Chang denote Lords Pingyuan and Mengchang; Chun and Ling denote Lords Chunshen and Xinling—all famed for hosting retainers across the realm.

sentences.zh: 若乃觀其四郊，浮游近縣，則南望杜﹑霸，北眺五陵，名都對郭，邑居相承，英俊之域，黻冕所興，冠蓋如雲，七相五公。

translations.literal: If then observe its four suburbs—float roam neighboring counties—then south gaze Du—Ba—north gaze Five Mausoleums—famed capitals face outer walls—settlements dwell mutually succeed—heroic outstanding region—fu-mian arise—caps covers like clouds—seven chancellors five dukes.
translations.idiomatic: Survey the capital's outskirts: south lie Duling and Baling; north the Five Imperial Tombs—great towns hugging ward walls, neighborhoods linked in chains—the cradle of nobility whose carriage canopies crowd like clouds: seven chief ministers and five senior dukes.

sentences.zh: [一]與乎州郡之豪桀，五都之貨殖，三選七遷，充奉陵邑，蓋以強幹弱枝，隆上都而觀萬國。

translations.literal: [One] Together with regional champions—Five Capitals' merchants—triple selections seven relocations—fill serve mausoleum settlements—thereby strengthen trunk weaken branches—elevate upper capital—awe myriad states.
translations.idiomatic: [One] Proud heroes from every province and the Five Metropolises' merchants thrice selected and seven times relocated people the tomb counties—fortifying the core while thinning branches, glorifying the capital to overawe the realm.

sentences.zh: [二]封畿之內，厥土千里，逴犖諸夏，兼其所有。

translations.literal: [Two] Within royal domain—its soil thousand li—towering among Zhongxia—combine its possesses.
translations.idiomatic: [Two] Within the royal thousand-li belt Qin towers over the Central States and annexes what they hold.

sentences.zh: [三]其陽則崇山隱天，幽林穹谷，陸海珍藏，藍田美玉，商﹑洛緣其隈，鄠﹑杜濱其足，[四]源泉灌注，陂池交屬，竹林果園，芳草甘木，郊野之富，號曰近蜀。

translations.literal: [Three] Its yang side—lofty mountains hide sky—dark forests vaulted valleys—land-sea hidden treasures—Lantian fine jade—Shang—Luo trace bends—Hu—Du shore feet—[four] spring heads pour irrigation—ponds intersect belong—bamboo groves fruit orchards—fragrant herbs sweet trees—suburb wilds' wealth—styled called Near Shu.
translations.idiomatic: [Three] Sunward lie peaks that veil the sky, shadowed forests and sunken glens, treasures of hill and marsh, Lantian jade; the Shang and Luo hug their bends while Hu and Du spread at their feet; [four] springs feed chained reservoirs, bamboo groves and orchards perfume the belt—wealth rivaling Shu borderlands.

sentences.zh: [五]其陰則冠以九嵕，陪以甘泉，乃有靈宮起乎其中。

translations.literal: [Five] Its yin side—crowned by Nine Peaks—attended by Sweet Springs—then spirit palaces rise therein.
translations.idiomatic: [Five] Shadowward Nine Peaks crown the horizon with Sweet Springs Palace companion—spirit halls rising among them.

sentences.zh: 秦﹑漢之所極觀，淵﹑雲之所頌歎，於是乎存焉。

translations.literal: Qin—Han utmost viewed—Yuan—Yun hymn praised—hereupon remain therein.
translations.idiomatic: Grand spectacles Qin and Han prized and odes Wang Bao and Yang Xiong sang still stand here.

sentences.zh: [六]下有鄭﹑白之沃，衣食之源，堤封五萬，疆埸綺分，溝塍刻鏤，原隰龍鱗，決渠降雨，荷臿成雲，五穀垂穎，桑麻敷棻。

translations.literal: [Six] Below have Zheng—Bai's fertile—clothing food source—embanked fifty thousand—border fields brocade divide—ditch ridges carved engrave—highland marsh dragon scales—open canals summon rain—carry spades become cloud—five grains hang ears—mulberry hemp spread fragrance.
translations.idiomatic: [Six] Below lie Zheng and Bai's irrigated fields—cloth and grain heartland—fifty thousand qing stitched like brocade; ditches carve patterns; wetlands scale like dragon scales; canals pour rain from shovels raised like clouds; grain bows heavy; mulberry and hemp perfume the air.

sentences.zh: [七]東郊則有通溝大漕，潰渭洞河，泛舟山東，控引淮﹑湖，與海通波。

translations.literal: [Seven] East suburb then has connecting trench great canal—burst Wei penetrate Yellow—float boats mountain east—draw Huai—Lakes—with sea join waves.
translations.idiomatic: [Seven] Eastward great trenches link Wei and Yellow River traffic—convoys sail Shandong, Huai and Lakes feeding waves that reach the sea.

sentences.zh: [八]西郊則有上囿禁苑，林麓藪澤，陂池連乎蜀﹑漢，繚以周牆，四百餘里，離宮別館，三十六所，神池靈沼，往往而在。

translations.literal: [Eight] West suburb then has supreme preserve forbidden park—forest foothill marsh swamp—ponds chain toward Shu—Han—girdled by circuit wall—four hundred-plus li—detached palace separate lodges—thirty-six places—spirit pools numinous ponds—often often exist.
translations.idiomatic: [Eight] Westward stretch the imperial hunting parks—timbered hills and marshes whose ponds reach toward Shu and Han within four hundred li of wall—thirty-six detached halls and spirit pools dotting the preserve.

sentences.zh: [九] 其中乃有九真之麟，大宛之馬，黃支之犀，條枝之鳥，踰櫫□，越巨海，殊方異類，至三萬里。

translations.literal: [Nine] Within then have Jiuzhen's unicorn—Ferghana's horses—Huangzhi's rhinos—Tiaozhi's birds—cross dense forests—span vast seas—different regions strange kinds—reach thirty thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: [Nine] The parks harbor unicorns from Jiuzhen, Ferghana horses, Huangzhi rhinos, Tiaozhi birds—crossing forests and seas—exotics from thirty thousand li away.

translations.literal: [Ten]
translations.idiomatic: Section marker ten (variant manuscripts omit additional flourish).

sentences.zh: 注[一]浮游謂周流也。

translations.literal: Commentary [one]: Float roam means circuit flow.
translations.idiomatic: Note: "Roaming" means touring the outskirts.

sentences.zh: 杜﹑霸謂杜陵﹑霸陵，在城南，故南望也。

translations.literal: Du—Ba mean Duling—Baling—in city south—therefore south gaze.
translations.idiomatic: Duling and Baling lie south—hence "south gaze."

sentences.zh: 五陵謂長陵﹑安陵﹑陽陵﹑茂陵﹑平陵，在渭北，故北眺也。

translations.literal: Five Mausoleums mean Changling—Anling—Yangling—Maoling—Pingling—north of Wei—therefore north gaze.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Imperial Tombs north of the Wei explain "north gaze."

sentences.zh: 並徙人以置縣邑，故雲名都對郭。

translations.literal: Together relocate people to establish counties settlements—therefore cloud famed capitals face outer walls.
translations.idiomatic: Settled migrants formed counties hugging the outer wards.

sentences.zh: 蒼頡篇曰：「黻，綬也。

translations.literal: Cangjie Chapter says: "Fu—ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: The Cangjie glossary defines fu court ribbons.

sentences.zh: 冕，冠也。

translations.literal: Mian—crown.
translations.idiomatic: Mian denotes ceremonial crowns.

sentences.zh: 」其所徙者皆豪右﹑富貲﹑吏二千石，故多英俊冠蓋之人。

translations.literal: " Those relocated were all mighty right—rich wealth—officials two thousand piculs—therefore many heroic handsome cap covers.
translations.idiomatic: Relocated magnates, rich households, and two-thousand-piculs officials filled the zone with noble carriages.

sentences.zh: 如雲，言多也。

translations.literal: Like clouds—speaks many.
translations.idiomatic: "Like clouds" stresses multitude.

sentences.zh: 詩曰：「出其東門，有女如雲。

translations.literal: Poetry says: "Exit its east gate—maids like clouds.
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Poetry compares crowds to clouds outside the east gate.

sentences.zh: 」七相謂丞相車千秋，長陵人，黃霸﹑王商，並杜陵人也，韋賢﹑平當﹑魏相﹑王嘉，並平陵人也。

translations.literal: " Seven chancellors mean Chancellor Che Qianqiu—Changling man—Huang Ba—Wang Shang—both Duling men—Wei Xian—Ping Dang—Wei Xiang—Wang Jia—both Pingling men.
translations.idiomatic: The seven chancellors: Che Qianqiu of Changling; Huang Ba and Wang Shang of Duling; Wei Xian, Ping Dang, Wei Xiang, and Wang Jia of Pingling.

sentences.zh: 五公謂田蚡為太尉，長陵人，張安世為大司馬，朱博為司空，並杜陵人，平晏為司徒，韋賞為大司馬，並平陵人也。

translations.literal: Five dukes mean Tian Fen as grand commandant—Changling man—Zhang Anshi as marshal—Zhu Bo as minister of works—both Duling men—Ping Yan as minister of education—Wei Shang as marshal—both Pingling men.
translations.idiomatic: The five dukes: Tian Fen (grand commandant) and Zhang Anshi (marshal) of Changling and Duling; Zhu Bo (minister of works); Ping Yan (minister of education); Wei Shang (marshal) of Pingling.

sentences.zh: 注[二]前書音義曰：「五都謂洛陽﹑邯鄲﹑臨淄﹑宛﹑成都也。

translations.literal: Commentary [two]: Former book sounds meanings says: "Five Capitals mean Luoyang—Handan—Linzi—Wan—Chengdu.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The Five Capitals are Luoyang, Handan, Linzi, Wan, and Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 」三選，選三等之人，謂徙吏二千石及高貲富人及豪桀並兼之家於諸陵，蓋以強幹弱枝，非獨為奉山園也。

translations.literal: " Triple selection—select three grades persons—means relocate officials two thousand piculs and high wealth rich men and heroic predatory households to various mausoleums—thereby strengthen trunk weaken branches—not only for serving hill gardens.
translations.idiomatic: Triple selection moves two-thousand-piculs officials, magnates, and powerful clans to tomb counties—to strengthen the capital, not merely to tend imperial tombs.

sentences.zh: 見前書。

translations.literal: Seen in former book.
translations.idiomatic: See the History of Western Han.

sentences.zh: 自元帝已後不遷，故唯七焉。

translations.literal: From Emperor Yuan onward not relocate—therefore only seven.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Yuan stopped relocations only seven waves occurred.

sentences.zh: 爾雅曰：「觀，指示也。

translations.literal: Erya says: "Guan—point show.
translations.idiomatic: The Erya glosses guan as "to survey" or "show forth."

sentences.zh: 」「選」或為「徙」，義亦通。

translations.literal: " "Select" sometimes written "migrate"—meaning also connects.
translations.idiomatic: Some editions read xuan as xi for relocation—either gloss fits.

sentences.zh: 注[三]前書曰：「秦地沃野千里，人以富饒。

translations.literal: Commentary [three]: Former book says: "Qin territory fertile wild thousand li—people thereby rich abundant.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The History of Western Han praises Qin's thousand-li fertile basin and prosperous households.

sentences.zh: 」逴犖猶超絕也。

translations.literal: " Zhuoluo means transcend surpass.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuoluo means standing far above others.

sentences.zh: 逴音卓。

translations.literal: Zhuo pronounced like zhuo.
translations.idiomatic: Read zhuo like zhuo "table."

sentences.zh: 犖音呂角反。

translations.literal: Luo sound lü-jue fan.
translations.idiomatic: Luo is read lü-jue reversed.

sentences.zh: 諸夏謂中國也。

translations.literal: Zhongxia means Central States.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhongxia" denotes the Central States.

sentences.zh: 注[四]穹谷，深谷。

translations.literal: Commentary [four]: Vaulted valley—deep valley.
translations.idiomatic: Note: "Vaulted valleys" means steep ravines.

sentences.zh: 東方朔曰：「漢興，去三河之地，止灞﹑滻之西，都涇﹑渭之南，此謂天下陸海之地也。

translations.literal: Dongfang Shuo says: "Han arose—left Three Rivers territory—stopped Ba-Chan's west—capitalized Jing-Wei's south—this called realm land-sea territory.
translations.idiomatic: Dongfang Shuo observed that Han abandoned the Three Rivers heartland for the corridor west of the Ba and Chan and south of the Jing and Wei—a "dry sea" of inexhaustible wealth.

sentences.zh: 」范子計然曰：「玉出藍田。

translations.literal: " Fanzi Jinran says: "Jade emerges Lantian.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Li's Jinran adds that fine jade comes from Lantian.

sentences.zh: 」商及上洛皆縣名。

translations.literal: " Shang and Shangluo both county names.
translations.idiomatic: Shang and Shangluo are county seats.

sentences.zh: 隈，山曲也。

translations.literal: Wei—mountain bend.
translations.idiomatic: Wei means a mountain's inner bend.

sentences.zh: 濱猶近也。

translations.literal: Bin means near.
translations.idiomatic: Bin glosses as "alongside."

sentences.zh: 鄠﹑杜，二縣名，近南山之足。

translations.literal: Hu—Du—two county names—near southern mountain's feet.
translations.idiomatic: Hu and Du counties hug the southern foothills.

sentences.zh: 爾雅云：「麓，山足也。」

translations.literal: Erya says: "Lu—mountain foot."
translations.idiomatic: The Erya defines "foot" as the mountain base.

sentences.zh: 注[五]孔安國注尚書曰：「澤障曰陂，停水曰池。

translations.literal: Commentary [five]: Kong Anguo commentary Documents says: "Marsh barrier called bei—stopped water called chi.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Kong Anguo defines diked marshes as bei and standing water as chi.

sentences.zh: 」前書曰：「巴﹑蜀土地肥美，有山林竹樹蔬食果實之饒。

translations.literal: " Former book says: "Ba—Shu earth fertile beautiful—have mountain forest bamboo tree vegetable food fruit abundance.
translations.idiomatic: The History praises Ba-Shu's lush forests and orchards.

sentences.zh: 」今南山亦有之，與巴﹑蜀相類，故曰近蜀。

translations.literal: " Now southern mountain also has them—with Ba—Shu similar—therefore called Near Shu.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhongnan foothills rival Shu—hence "near Shu."

sentences.zh: 爾雅曰：「邑外曰郊，郊外曰野。」

translations.literal: Erya says: "Outside settlement called suburb—outside suburb called wild."
translations.idiomatic: The Erya distinguishes suburbs from wilds beyond.

sentences.zh: 注[六]陰謂北也。

translations.literal: Commentary [six]: Yin means north.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The shaded or "yin" side faces north.

sentences.zh: 九嵕山尤高峻，故稱冠雲。

translations.literal: Nine-Zhao mountain especially high steep—therefore called crown clouds.
translations.idiomatic: Nine-Zhao Peak towers highest—"crowning the clouds."

sentences.zh: 甘泉山在雲陽北，秦始皇於上置林光宮，漢又起甘泉宮﹑益壽﹑延壽館﹑通天台，故云「秦﹑漢之所極觀」。

translations.literal: Sweet Springs mountain in Yunyang north—Qin First Emperor on it placed Lenguang Palace—Han again raised Sweet Springs Palace—Yishou—Yanshou lodges—Penetrating Heaven Terrace—therefore cloud "Qin—Han utmost viewed.
translations.idiomatic: North of Yunyang Sweet Springs Mountain bore Qin's Lenguang Palace and Han's Sweet Springs complex with longevity halls and sky-piercing towers—the spectacle Qin and Han prized.

sentences.zh: 王□字子泉，作甘泉頌，楊子雲作甘泉賦，故云「泉﹑雲頌歎」。

translations.literal: Wang Bao styled Ziquan—composed Sweet Springs hymn—Yang Ziyun composed Sweet Springs fu—therefore cloud "Springs—Cloud hymn praise.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ziquan's hymn and Yang Xiong's Sweet Springs fu supply the "springs and cloud" praise.

sentences.zh: 注[七]史記曰：「韓使水工鄭國說秦，令引涇水為渠，傍北山，東注洛，溉田四萬余頃，名曰鄭國渠。

translations.literal: Commentary [seven]: Records of the Grand Historian says: "Han sent hydraulic engineer Zheng Guo persuade Qin—ordered lead Jing water make canal—along north mountains—east pour Luo—irrigate fields forty thousand-plus qing—named Zheng Guo Canal.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Han dispatched engineer Zheng Guo to persuade Qin to divert the Jing east toward the Luo along the northern hills—irrigating over forty thousand qing—the Zheng Guo Canal.

sentences.zh: 」武帝時，趙中大夫白公奏穿渠引涇水，首起谷口，尾入櫟陽，溉田四千餘頃，因名白渠。

translations.literal: " Emperor Wu times—Zhao court counsellor Bai Gong memorialized bore canal lead Jing water—head rise valley mouth—tail enter Yueyang—irrigate fields four thousand-plus qing—therefore named Bai Canal.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu Counsellor Bai Gong opened the Bai Canal from Jingkou to Yueyang—four thousand qing.

sentences.zh: 時人歌之曰：「田於何所？

translations.literal: People of time sang saying: "Fields at what place?
translations.idiomatic: A contemporary song asked: "Where do we farm?"

sentences.zh: 池陽谷口。

translations.literal: Chiyang valley mouth.
translations.idiomatic: At Chiyang's valley mouth."

sentences.zh: 鄭國在前，白渠起後。

translations.literal: Zheng Guo front—Bai Canal rose after.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Guo's canal came first; Bai's followed.

sentences.zh: 舉臿為雲，決渠為雨。

translations.literal: Raise spades become clouds—open canals become rain.
translations.idiomatic: Spades rose like clouds; opening sluices poured rain.

sentences.zh: 涇水一石，其泥數鬥。

translations.literal: Jing water one picul—its mud several dou.
translations.idiomatic: Each picul of Jing water carried several dou of fertile silt.

sentences.zh: 且溉且糞，長我禾黍。

translations.literal: And irrigate and manure—grow our grain millet.
translations.idiomatic: Irrigation and muck together fattened millet and panicled grain.

sentences.zh: 衣食京師，億萬之口。

translations.literal: Clothe feed capital—hundred-million mouths.
translations.idiomatic: They clothed and fed countless mouths in the capital.

sentences.zh: 」前書曰：「天子畿方千里，堤封百萬井。

translations.literal: " Former book says: "Son of Heaven domain square thousand li—embanked seal million wells.
translations.idiomatic: The History counts the royal thousand-li belt as a million well-fields.

sentences.zh: 」音義曰：「堤謂積土為封限也，音丁奚反。

translations.literal: " Sounds meanings says: "Di means pile earth make sealed boundary—sound ding-xi fan.
translations.idiomatic: Glossators define di as earthen boundary markers—read ding-xi reversed.

sentences.zh: 」廣雅曰：「埸，界也。

translations.literal: " Guangya says: "Yi—boundary.
translations.idiomatic: The Guangya glosses yi as field borders.

sentences.zh: 」音亦。

translations.literal: " Sound yi.
translations.idiomatic: Pronounced yi.

sentences.zh: 周禮曰：「夫閒有遂，十夫有溝。

translations.literal: Zhou Rites says: "Between husbands has ditch—ten husbands has trench.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou Rites prescribes ditches between plots and trenches every ten plots.

sentences.zh: 」說文曰：「塍，田畦也。

translations.literal: " Shuowen says: "Cheng—field ridge.
translations.idiomatic: The Shuowen defines cheng as raised field ridges.

sentences.zh: 」塍音繩。

translations.literal: " Cheng sound sheng.
translations.idiomatic: Read cheng like sheng "rope."

sentences.zh: 刻鏤謂交錯如鏤也。

translations.literal: Carved engraved means interwoven like carving.
translations.idiomatic: The intricate ditch pattern resembles carved fretwork.

sentences.zh: 爾雅曰：「高平曰原，下濕曰隰。

translations.literal: Erya says: "High level called yuan—low wet called xi.
translations.idiomatic: The Erya distinguishes high plains from marshy lows.

sentences.zh: 」言如龍鱗之五色也。

translations.literal: " Speaking like dragon scales' five colors.
translations.idiomatic: The patchwork gleams like dragon scales.

sentences.zh: 五穀，黍、稷、菽、麥、稻也。

translations.literal: Five grains—millet—panicled millet—beans—wheat—rice.
translations.idiomatic: The five grains: millet, panicled millet, beans, wheat, rice.

sentences.zh: *[小]*爾雅曰：「禾穗謂之穎。

translations.literal: *[Minor]* Erya says: "Grain ear called ying.
translations.idiomatic: The Minor Erya calls grain heads ying.

sentences.zh: 」*[小]*爾雅曰：「敷，布也。

translations.literal: " *[Minor]* Erya says: "Fu—spread.
translations.idiomatic: The Minor Erya glosses fu as "to spread."

sentences.zh: 」棻，茂盛也，音芬。

translations.literal: " Fen—lush flourishing—sound fen.
translations.idiomatic: Fen means luxuriant growth—read fen.

sentences.zh: 注[八]漕，水運也。

translations.literal: Commentary [eight]: Cao—water transport.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Cao denotes canal shipping.

sentences.zh: 蒼頡篇曰：「潰，傍決也。

translations.literal: Cangjie Chapter says: "Kui—side burst.
translations.idiomatic: The Cangjie glossary defines kui as breaking out sideways into rivers.

sentences.zh: 」前書武帝穿漕渠通渭。

translations.literal: " Former book Emperor Wu dug transport canal connect Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu cut transport canals linking to the Wei.

sentences.zh: 史記曰：「滎陽下引河東南為鴻溝，以與淮、泗會。」

translations.literal: Records of the Grand Historian says: "Xingyang below drew Yellow River southeast as Grand Canal—to join Huai—Si."
translations.idiomatic: Sima Qian describes diverting the Yellow east from Xingyang through the Grand Canal to Huai and Si.

sentences.zh: 注[九]上囿謂林苑也。

translations.literal: Commentary [nine]: Supreme preserve means forest park.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The upper hunting park is the imperial preserve.

sentences.zh: 谷梁傳曰：「林屬於山為麓。

translations.literal: Guliang Tradition says: "Forest attaching mountain is foothill.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Commentary defines foothills where forests cling to mountains.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注周禮曰：「澤無水曰藪。

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan commentary Zhou Rites says: "Marsh without water called sou.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan calls dry marshes sou.

sentences.zh: 」繚猶繞也，音了。

translations.literal: " Liao means wind around—sound liao.
translations.idiomatic: Liao means "encircle"—read liao.

sentences.zh: 三輔黃圖曰：「上林有建章、承光等一十一宮，平樂、繭觀等二十五，凡三十六所。

translations.literal: Yellow Chart of Three Adjuncts says: "Upper Grove has Jianzhang—Chengguang etc. eleven palaces—Pingle—Jian Guan etc. twenty-five—altogether thirty-six places.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Chart lists thirty-six halls including eleven major palaces such as Jianzhang and twenty-five lodges such as Pingle.

sentences.zh: 」三秦記曰：「昆明池中有神池，通白鹿原。

translations.literal: " Record of Three Qin says: "Kunming Pond has spirit pool—connects White Deer Plain.
translations.idiomatic: Kunming Pond held spirit pools linked to White Deer Plain.

sentences.zh: 」詩曰：「王在靈沼。」

translations.literal: " Poetry says: "King at Spirit Pond."
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Poetry celebrates the king at Spirit Pond."

sentences.zh: 注[一０]宣帝詔曰：「九真郡獻奇獸。

translations.literal: Commentary [ten]: Emperor Xuan edict says: "Jiuzhen commandery presented strange beasts.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Emperor Xuan's edict mentions Jiuzhen presenting exotic animals.

sentences.zh: 」晉灼漢書注云：「駒形，麟色，牛角。

translations.literal: " Jin Zhuo Han Documents commentary says: "Foal shape—unicorn color—ox horn.
translations.idiomatic: Jin Zhuo describes the beast as foal-shaped, unicorn-colored, ox-horned.

sentences.zh: 」武帝時，李廣利斬大宛王首，獲汗血馬來。

translations.literal: " Emperor Wu times—Li Guangli cut Ferghana king head—obtained sweat-blood horses came.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guangli brought Ferghana's sweat-blood horses after defeating its king.

sentences.zh: 又黃支國自三萬里貢生犀。

translations.literal: Again Huangzhi state from thirty thousand li tribute live rhinos.
translations.idiomatic: Huangzhi sent live rhinos from thirty thousand li away.

sentences.zh: 條支國臨西海，有大鳥，卵如甕。

translations.literal: Tiaozhi state borders western sea—has great birds—eggs like urns.
translations.idiomatic: Tiaozhi on the western sea breeds giant birds with urn-sized eggs.

sentences.zh: 條支與安息接，武帝時，安息國發使來獻之。

translations.literal: Tiaozhi joins Parthia—Emperor Wu times—Parthia state dispatched envoys came presented them.
translations.idiomatic: Bordering Parthia, it sent tribute envoys under Emperor Wu.

sentences.zh: 又曰：「櫫□山高二千五百里。

translations.literal: Again says: "Barrier mountains height two thousand five hundred li.
translations.idiomatic: Another account cites barrier mountains twenty-five hundred li high.

sentences.zh: 」並見前書。

translations.literal: " Together seen former book.
translations.idiomatic: See the History of Western Han.

sentences.zh: 其宮室也，體象乎天地，經緯乎陰陽，據坤靈之正位，放*

translations.literal: Their palace chambers—body image Heaven Earth—warp weft Yin Yang—occupy Kun spirit's correct position—model after *
translations.idiomatic: The palace architecture bodies forth Heaven and Earth, threads Yin and Yang, sits true on earth's numinous axis, patterning itself after

translations.literal: (Tai)
translations.idiomatic: (Tai, completing Taishi).

sentences.zh: **[太]*、紫之圓方。

translations.literal: **[Grand]* Wei—Purple's round square.
translations.idiomatic: It traced the spherical Heaven of Grand Tenuity and the squared Purple Forbidden enclosure.*

sentences.zh: [一]樹中天之華闕，豐冠山之朱堂，因瑰材而究奇，抗應龍之虹梁，列棼橑以布翼，荷棟桴而高驤。

translations.literal: [One] Plant mid-heaven's splendid towers—rich crown mountain's vermilion halls—by rare timber pursue marvel—raise responding dragon's rainbow beams—array layered rafters spread wings—bear ridge beams soaring high.
translations.idiomatic: [One] They raised sky-scraping towers and vermilion halls crowning hills, wrought wonders from precious timber, rainbow beams like dragons arched, rafters fanned like wings, ridgepoles vaulting skyward.

sentences.zh: [二]雕玉瑱以居楹，裁金璧以飾璫，發五色之渥采，光爓朗以景彰。

translations.literal: [Two] Carve jade zhen to occupy pillars—cut gold jades to ornament dang—emit five colors' rich hues—light flame bright thereby shine manifest.
translations.idiomatic: [Two] Jade inlays studded the columns, gold disks trimmed the tiles, five-colored richness blazed, flame-bright gloss flashing everywhere.

sentences.zh: [三]於是左*

translations.literal: [Three] Thereupon left—
translations.idiomatic: [Three] Westward rose the stair-flanked ascent—

translations.literal: (lacuna)
translations.idiomatic: (Manuscript gap in one graph.)

sentences.zh: **[腆]*右平，重軒三階，閨房周通，門闥洞開，列鐘虡於中庭，立金人於端闈，仍增崖而衡閾，臨峻路而啟扉。

translations.literal: **[Level]* right level—double eaves three stairs—inner chambers round penetrate—doors openings gaping open—array bell racks in central courtyard—stand golden men at gate openings—still increase cliffs balance thresholds—overlook steep roads open doors.
translations.idiomatic: To the east the pavement stayed level; double galleries and triple stairways joined inner quarters; gates opened on bell stands and bronze Colossi in the vestibules; cliff-like sills spanned steep ramps.

sentences.zh: [四] 徇以離殿別寢，承以崇台閒豫，煥若列星，紫宮是環。

translations.literal: [Four] Circle with detached halls separate lodges—support with lofty terraces leisure ease—brilliant like arrayed stars—Purple Palace thereby ring.
translations.idiomatic: [Four] Detached halls and resting lodges ring the core on lofty terraces—sparkling like constellations orbiting the Purple Palace.

sentences.zh: [五]清涼宣溫，神仙長年，金華玉堂，白虎麒麟，區宇若茲，不可殫論。

translations.literal: [Five] Clear Cool—Declare Warm—Immortal Gods Long Years—Golden Splendor Jade Hall—White Tiger Qilin—regions thus—cannot exhaust discuss.
translations.idiomatic: [Five] Halls named Clear Cool, Declare Warm, Immortals' Longevity, Golden Splendor, Jade Hall, White Tiger, Qilin—the compound cannot be catalogued.

sentences.zh: [六]增盤業峨，登降照爛，殊形詭制，每各異觀，乘茵步輦，唯所息宴。

translations.literal: [Six] tiered coils lofty—ascend descend blazing bright—different shapes strange designs—each different view—ride cushion walk palanquin—only rest feast.
translations.idiomatic: [Six] Terraced stacks soar and flare—every tower oddly wrought—guests ride cushions or palanquins wherever they halt to feast.

sentences.zh: [七]後宮則有掖庭椒房，后妃之室，合歡增成，安處常寧，□若椒風，披香發越，蘭林蕙草，鴛鸞飛翔之列。

translations.literal: [Seven] Rear palace then has Yeeting Pepper-Chamber—empress consorts' chambers—Together Joy Layered Complete—Peaceful Dwelling Ever Calm—like Pepper Breeze—Spread Fragrance Radiant Leap—Orchid Grove Sweet Grass—mandarin ducks simurghs soaring ranks.
translations.idiomatic: [Seven] Inner quarters include Pepper Chambers for consorts—halls named Together Joy, Layered Bloom, Peace Abode, Pepper Breeze, Spread Fragrance—orchard lanes where paired birds seem to soar.

sentences.zh: [八]昭陽特盛，隆乎孝成，屋不呈材，牆不露形，裛以藻繡，絡以綸連，隨侯明月，錯落其閒，金釭銜璧，是為列錢，翡翠火齊，流耀含英，懸黎垂棘，夜光在焉。

translations.literal: [Eight] Zhaoyang especially flourished—elevated Emperor Xiaocheng—roof not display timber—wall not expose form—wrap with embroidered designs—net with silk cords—Sui marquis bright moon—scattered among—golden collar holds jade—this is array coins—kingfisher fire blend—flow radiance hold brilliance—suspended li hanging ji—night light therein.
translations.idiomatic: [Eight] Zhaoyang Hall peaked under Cheng—timbers hidden, walls seamless behind brocade hangings—pearls like Suihou's pearls and moon-disks strewn among bronze bosses chained like coins—jade and coral blaze—gems glow night-bright.

sentences.zh: [九]於是玄墀扣切，玉階彤庭，□磩采致，琳□青熒，珊瑚碧樹，周阿而生。

translations.literal: [Nine] Thereupon dark courtyard carved polish—jade stairs vermilion court—inlaid qi splendid polish—lin jade green gleam—coral jade trees—round bend thereby grow.
translations.idiomatic: [Nine] Black lacquered courts, jade stairs in vermilion halls—inlaid panels gleam—blue-green columns flare—coral "trees" branch along curving eaves.

sentences.zh: [一０]紅羅颯纚，綺組繽紛，精曜華燭，俯仰如神。

translations.literal: [Ten] Red gauze rustling trailing—brocade ribbons riot—spirit blaze splendid candles—bow rise like gods.
translations.idiomatic: [Ten] Red gauze ripples, patterned ribbons riot—gems flare like sacred lamps shifting light at every glance.

sentences.zh: [一一] 後宮之號，十有四位，窈窕繁華，更盛迭貴，處乎斯列者，蓋以百數。

translations.literal: [Eleven] Rear palace's titles—fourteen ranks—graceful splendid flourish—alternately flourish alternate noble—dwell in these ranks—probably hundred count.
translations.idiomatic: [Eleven] Fourteen ranks of inner palaces—beauties rivaling one another—number in the hundreds.

sentences.zh: [一二] 左右廷中，朝堂百僚之位，蕭曹魏邴，謀謨乎其上。

translations.literal: [Twelve] Left-right court middle—morning hall hundred officials' positions—Xiao—Cao—Wei—Bing—plans counsels upon above.
translations.idiomatic: [Twelve] In the outer court myriad officers stood—Xiao He, Cao Shen, Wei Xiang, Bing Ji—debating policy above.

sentences.zh: [一三]佐命則垂統，輔翼則成化，流大漢之愷悌，蕩亡秦之毒螫。

translations.literal: [Thirteen] Assist mandate then hang thread—help wings then complete transformation—flow Great Han's kindness—sweep dead Qin's poison sting.
translations.idiomatic: [Thirteen] Ministers sustain the Mandate and transmit governance—Great Han's mercy washes away Qin's cruelty.

sentences.zh: [一四]故令斯人揚樂和之聲，作畫一之歌，功德著於祖宗，膏澤洽於黎庶。

translations.literal: [Fourteen] Therefore order these people raise harmonious music sounds—compose unify-one songs—merit virtue manifest at ancestors—fat dew permeate common folk.
translations.idiomatic: [Fourteen] They stirred harmonious hymns and songs of unity—merit shining on the ancestral temples, bounty soaking the people.

sentences.zh: [一五]又有天祿石渠，典籍之府，命夫諄誨故老，名儒師傅，講論乎六蓺，稽合乎同異。

translations.literal: [Fifteen] Again have Celestial Blessing Stone Canal—classic texts' treasury—order husbands earnest instruct aged elders—famed Confucians masters—discourse six arts—investigate harmonize same different.
translations.idiomatic: [Fifteen] Celestial Archive and Stone Canal libraries gathered classics—summoning elder scholars to expound the six arts and reconcile divergent glosses.

sentences.zh: [一六]又有承明金馬，著作之庭，大雅宏達，於茲為腢，元元本本，周見洽聞，啟發篇章，校理秘文。

translations.literal: [Sixteen] Again have Chenming Golden Horse—composition courtyard—great elegance vast achieved—here become gather—root root stem stem—pervasive see broad hear—open develop chapters—collate manage secret texts.
translations.idiomatic: [Sixteen] Chenming Gate and Golden Horse Stable housed compilers—great scholars thronged there tracing origins and variants, editing palace manuscripts.

sentences.zh: [一七]周以鉤陳之位，□以嚴更之署，總禮官之甲科，腢百郡之廉孝。

translations.literal: [Seventeen] Circle Hook Array positions—supplement by strict watch offices—gather ritual officials' top grade—gather hundred commanderies' integrity filial.
translations.idiomatic: [Seventeen] Hook Array guards ringed night-watch offices; ritual examiners ranked top graduates and summoned county nominees for integrity and filial piety.

sentences.zh: [一八]虎賁贅衣，閹尹閽寺，陛戟百重，各有攸司。

translations.literal: [Eighteen] Tiger Brave redundant robes—eunuch directors gatekeepers—stairs halberds hundred layers—each has charge.
translations.idiomatic: [Eighteen] Tiger guards and robe-clad attendants, eunuch stewards and gatekeepers—rank on rank of halberds at the stairs—each corps with its duty.

sentences.zh: [一九]周廬千列，徼道綺錯。

translations.literal: [Nineteen] Circuit barracks thousand rows—patrol lanes brocade cross.
translations.idiomatic: [Nineteen] Guard barracks line the perimeter; patrol lanes lace like brocade.

sentences.zh: [二０] 輦路經營，修塗飛閣。

translations.literal: [Twenty] Palanquin roads measured—long roads flying galleries.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty] Imperial avenues stretch beneath soaring arcades.

sentences.zh: [二一]自未央而連桂宮，北彌明光而戇長樂，陵墱道而超西墉，混建章而外屬，設璧門之鳳闕，上柧稜而棲金雀。

translations.literal: [Twenty-one] From Weiyang yet link Osmanthus Palace—north reach Mingguang yet oppose Changle—cross elevated road surpass western wall—blend Jianzhang outward attach—establish jade gate's phoenix towers—above corner ridges perch golden sparrows.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-one] Corridors joined Weiyang to Osmanthus, stretched north through Mingguang toward Changle—raised roads vaulted western walls into Jianzhang—jade gates mounted phoenix towers whose roof ridges bore golden birds.

sentences.zh: [二二]內則別風之嶕嶢，眇麗巧而竦擢，張千門而立萬戶，順陰陽以開闔。

translations.literal: [Twenty-two] Within then Separate Wind's towering—subtle beautiful clever yet soar rise—stretch thousand gates establish ten thousand doors—accord Yin Yang open close.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-two] Inside rose Separate Wind's dizzy stacks—thousands of gates and myriad doors opening with cosmic rhythm.

sentences.zh: [二三]爾乃正殿崔巍，層構厥高，臨乎未央，經駘蕩而出馺娑，洞枍詣與天梁，上反宇以蓋戴，激日景而納光。

translations.literal: [Twenty-three] Then main hall towering—layered structure its height—overlook Weiyang—pass Daichang exit Sasa—penetrate Yiqi with Tianliang—above inverted roof cover support—stimulate sun vista absorb light.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-three] The main hall loomed over Weiyang—corridors threaded Daichang and Sasa, Yiqi and Tianliang—inverted eaves caught sun-shafts.

sentences.zh: [二四]神明郁其特起，遂偃蹇而上躋，軼雲雨於太半，虹霓回帶於棼楣，雖輕迅與僄狡，猶愕眙而不敢階。

translations.literal: [Twenty-four] Spirit Bright luxuriant its special rise—thereupon lofty ascend—surpass cloud rain more than half—rainbow belt turning rafters—even swift nimble—still stare dare not climb.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-four] Spirit Bright Terrace rose sheer—surmounting clouds—rainbows seemed to ring its beams—even lithe climbers froze awestruck.

sentences.zh: [二五]攀井干而未半，目眴轉而意迷，捨欞檻而卻倚，若顛墜而復稽，魂怳怳以失度，巡迴塗而下低。

translations.literal: [Twenty-five] Climb well pavilion not half—eyes dizzy turn intent confused—leave lattice railing retreat lean—as topple fall yet pause—soul dizzy dizzy lose measure—circle path descend low.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-five] Halfway up the tower sight swims—clutching rails lest one fall—spirit reels until paths spiral downward.

sentences.zh: [二六]既懲懼於登望，降周流以彷徨，步甬道以縈紆，又杳窱而不見陽。

translations.literal: [Twenty-six] Already punish fear at ascend gaze—descend circuit flow wander—walk vaulted corridor wound twist—again dim remote not see yang.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-six] After vertigo they descended winding cloisters—dim corridors where sunlight vanished.

sentences.zh: [二七]排飛闥而上出，若遊目於天表，似無依*

translations.literal: [Twenty-seven] Push flying tower upward emerge—as roam eyes at heaven's surface—as without rely *
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-seven] Shooting through flying portals they seemed to scan heaven's rim—as if without support

translations.literal: (zhi)
translations.idiomatic: (the particle completing "without support")

translations.literal: **[And]* vast vast.
translations.idiomatic: —yet vast and boundless.*

sentences.zh: [二八]前唐中而後太液，攬滄海之湯湯，揚波濤於碣石，激神岳之嶈嶈，濫瀛洲與方壺，蓬萊起乎中央。

translations.literal: [Twenty-eight] Front Tangzhong rear Taiye—embrace vast sea surging—raise waves billows at Jieshi—stir divine peaks towering—overflow Yingzhou with Fanghu—Penglai rises therein central.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-eight] Beyond Tangzhong Pool stretched Taiye—waves like blue seas crashing Jieshi—sacred peaks loom—isles Yingzhou, Fanghu, Penglai rising mid-lake.

sentences.zh: [二九]於是靈草冬榮，神木叢生，巖峻崔崒，金石崢嶸。

translations.literal: [Twenty-nine] Thereupon spirit grass winter flourish—divine trees densely grow—crags steep towering—metal stone rugged.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-nine] Winter-green herbs and sacred groves crowded crags—rocks ringing like struck bronze.

sentences.zh: [三０]抗仙掌*

translations.literal: [Thirty] Raise immortal palm *
translations.idiomatic: [Thirty] They set up bronze immortal palms to catch the dew.

translations.literal: (yu)
translations.idiomatic: The gloss supplies yu, meaning "together with."

sentences.zh: **[以]*承露，擢雙立之金莖，軼埃壒之混濁，鮮顥氣之清英。

translations.literal: **[To]* catch dew—pull twin-standing golden stems—surpass dust mist turbidity—fresh vast qi clear brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: **[With these]** they caught the dew; paired golden pillars rose through turbid dust-haze into clear, radiant air.

sentences.zh: [三一]騁文成之丕誕，馳五利之所刑，庶松喬之腢類，時游從乎斯庭，實列仙之攸館，匪吾人之所寧。

translations.literal: [Thirty-one] Gallop Wencheng's grand sham—race Five Profits' executions—hope Pine-Qiao's companions—sometimes roam follow this court—truly host immortals' lodges—not common men's peace.
translations.idiomatic: [Thirty-one] Here shamans like Wencheng and condemned charlatans such as the Five Profits once raced—only immortal companions roam such courts—the realm of transcendents, no mortal rest.

sentences.zh: [三二]

translations.literal: [Thirty-two]
translations.idiomatic: Section marker thirty-two.

sentences.zh: 注[一]圓象天，方象地。

translations.literal: Comment [one] Round matches Heaven; square matches Earth.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [one]: The circle stands for Heaven and the square for Earth.

sentences.zh: 南北為經，東西為緯。

translations.literal: South-north as warp; east-west as weft.
translations.idiomatic: South and north form the warp; east and west form the weft.

sentences.zh: 楊雄司空箴曰：「普彼坤靈，侔天作合。

translations.literal: Yang Xiong's Admonition for the Minister of Works says: "Universally that earthly spirit—paired with Heaven to join.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xiong's *Admonition for the Minister of Works* says: "Everywhere that earthly spirit pairs itself with Heaven.

sentences.zh: 」放，像也。

translations.literal: " fang—means image.
translations.idiomatic: " *Fang* here means "to resemble" or "image."

sentences.zh: 太、紫謂太微、紫宮也。

translations.literal: Tai, zi refer to Taiwei, Zigong.
translations.idiomatic: "Tai" and "zi" denote the Taiwei asterism and the Purple Palace.

sentences.zh: 劉向七略曰：「明堂之制：內有太室，像紫宮；

translations.literal: Liu Xiang's Seven Summaries says: "Bright Hall's pattern: inside has Grand Chamber—images Zigong;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang's *Seven Summaries* says: "The Bright Hall's design places the Grand Chamber within, modeled on the Purple Palace;

sentences.zh: 南出明堂，像太微。

translations.literal: South exits Bright Hall—images Taiwei.
translations.idiomatic: where it opens southward it figures the Bright Hall, modeled on Taiwei.

sentences.zh: 」春秋合誠圖曰：「太微，其星十二，四方。

translations.literal: " Spring-Autumn Accord with Proofs Diagram says: "Taiwei—its stars twelve—four directions.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Spring and Autumn Accord with Proofs Diagram* says: "Taiwei has twelve stars in the four quarters.

sentences.zh: 」史記天官書曰：「環之匡□十二星，藩臣，皆曰紫宮。

translations.literal: " Records of the Historian Heavenly Offices Treatise says: "Surrounding its frame □ twelve stars—screen ministers—all called Zigong.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Records of the Grand Historian*, "Treatise on Celestial Offices," says: "Twelve stars ring its enclosure [lacuna]; they are the feudatories; all are styled the Purple Palace.

sentences.zh: 」是太微方而紫宮圓也。

translations.literal: " Thus Taiwei square yet Zigong round.
translations.idiomatic: " Hence Taiwei is square while the Purple Palace is round.

sentences.zh: 注[二]列子曰：「周穆王作中天之台。

translations.literal: Comment [two] Liezi says: "King Mu of Zhou built mid-Heaven tower.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [two]: *Liezi* says: "King Mu of Zhou raised a tower at the center of the sky."

sentences.zh: 」說文曰：「闕，門觀也。

translations.literal: " Shuowen says: "Que—gate-view.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Shuowen* says: "A *que* is a gate-tower for viewing."

sentences.zh: 」前書蕭何作東闕、北闕。

translations.literal: " Former Documents Xiao He made east que, north que.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Former Han History* records that Xiao He built the eastern and northern gate-towers.

sentences.zh: 豐，大也。

translations.literal: Feng—great.
translations.idiomatic: *Feng* means "great" or "lush."

sentences.zh: 冠山謂在山之上也。

translations.literal: Crown-mountain—means upon mountain's top.
translations.idiomatic: "Crowning the mountain" means sitting atop the hill.

sentences.zh: 埤蒼曰：「瑰瑋，珍奇也。

translations.literal: Piyang says: "Guiwei—rare wonder.
translations.idiomatic: The *Piyang* says: "*Guiwei* denotes rare and marvelous things."

sentences.zh: 」廣雅曰：「有翼曰應龍。

translations.literal: " Guangya says: "Having wings called responding dragon.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Guangya* says: "A dragon with wings is called a 'responding dragon.'"

sentences.zh: 」梁作應龍之形，而又曲如虹也。

translations.literal: " Beam made responding-dragon's shape—yet also curved like rainbow.
translations.idiomatic: " The crossbeam was fashioned like a responding dragon, sinuous as a rainbow.

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「棼，復屋之棟。

translations.literal: Shuowen says: "Fen—double-roof's ridge-beam.
translations.idiomatic: The *Shuowen* says: "*Fen* is the ridge-beam of a double-layered roof."

sentences.zh: 」橑，椽也。

translations.literal: " lao—rafter.
translations.idiomatic: " *Lao* means rafters."

sentences.zh: 翼，屋之四阿也。

translations.literal: Yi—roof's four corners.
translations.idiomatic: *Yi* here names the four corners or eaves of the roof.

sentences.zh: 荷，負也。

translations.literal: He—bear.
translations.idiomatic: *He* means to bear or carry on the shoulder.

sentences.zh: 驤，舉也。

translations.literal: Xiang—raise.
translations.idiomatic: *Xiang* means to lift or rear upward.

sentences.zh: 爾雅曰：「棟謂之桴。

translations.literal: Erya says: "Ridge-beam called fu.
translations.idiomatic: The *Erya* says: "The ridge-beam is called *fu.*"

sentences.zh: 」音浮。

translations.literal: " sound fu.
translations.idiomatic: " It is read like *fu* (float)."

sentences.zh: 注[三]廣雅曰：「磌，礩也。

translations.literal: Comment [three] Guangya says: "Tian—chopping block.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [three]: The *Guangya* says: "*Tian* is a base or plinth (*zhi*)."

sentences.zh: 」音田。

translations.literal: " sound tian.
translations.idiomatic: " It is read *tian* (field)."

sentences.zh: 「瑱」與「磌」通。

translations.literal: "Zhen" and "tian" connect.
translations.idiomatic: The graphs zhen and tian are used interchangeably.

sentences.zh: 楹，柱也。

translations.literal: Ying—pillar.
translations.idiomatic: *Ying* means a column.

sentences.zh: 雕玉為礩以承柱也。

translations.literal: Carve jade as zhi to bear pillar.
translations.idiomatic: Jade was carved into plinths to carry the columns.

sentences.zh: 上林賦曰：「華榱璧璫。

translations.literal: Forest Rhapsody says: "Ornate purlin jade dang.
translations.idiomatic: The *Rhapsody on the Shanglin Park* says: "Gilded purlins and jade dang-ends."

sentences.zh: 」韋昭注曰：「璫，榱頭也。

translations.literal: " Wei Zhao's comment says: "Dang—purlin head.
translations.idiomatic: " Wei Zhao's note says: "*Dang* is the purlin cap."

sentences.zh: 」渥，光潤也。

translations.literal: " wo—glossy moist.
translations.idiomatic: " *Wo* means glossy and moist."

sentences.zh: 爓音艷。

translations.literal: Yan sound yan (gorgeous).
translations.idiomatic: *Yan* is read like *yan* meaning brilliant or gorgeous.

sentences.zh: 注[四]摯虞決疑要注曰：「腆者為階級，平者以文磚相亞次也。

translations.literal: Comment [four] Zhi Yu's Essential Gloss on Resolving Doubts says: "Thick ones make stair-steps—flat ones use patterned bricks mutually tier.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [four]: Zhi Yu's *Essential Gloss on Resolving Doubts* says: "Raised courses form steps; level stretches lay patterned bricks in overlapping tiers."

sentences.zh: 」「域」亦作「腆」。

translations.literal: " "yu" is also written "tian".
translations.idiomatic: The graph for "domain" is sometimes written with the "thick" graph.

sentences.zh: 言階級勒腆然，音七則反。

translations.literal: Say stair-steps carved thick-like—sound qi ze fan.
translations.idiomatic: The steps are described as sharply stacked; the reading follows the *qi ze* fanqie.

sentences.zh: 王逸楚辭注曰：「軒，樓板也。

translations.literal: Wang Yi's Songs of Chu comment says: "Xuan—loft plank.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yi's commentary on the *Songs of Chu* says: "*Xuan* is the loft flooring."

sentences.zh: 」周禮夏後氏「世室九階」，鄭玄注云「南面三階，三面各二」也。

translations.literal: " Zhou Rites Xia clan "earth chamber nine steps"—Zheng Xuan comment says "south face three steps—three faces each two".
translations.idiomatic: " The *Zhou Rites* records the Xia "earth chamber" with nine steps; Zheng Xuan explains that three lie on the south and two on each of the other three sides.

sentences.zh: 爾雅曰：「宮中之門謂之闈，小者謂之閨。

translations.literal: Erya says: "Palace-inside's gate called wei—small called gui.
translations.idiomatic: The *Erya* says: "A gate inside the palace precinct is a *wei*; a smaller one is a *gui.*"

sentences.zh: 」廣以懸鐘也。

translations.literal: " Broad—used hang bells.
translations.idiomatic: " It is widened to suspend bells."

sentences.zh: 史記：「秦始皇收天下兵器，聚之咸陽，銷以為金人十二，置宮中。

translations.literal: Records: "Qin Shihuang gathered realm weapons—assembled Xianyang—melt for twelve bronze men—placed palace inside.
translations.idiomatic: The *Records* says: "The First Emperor of Qin collected the weapons of the realm at Xianyang, cast them into twelve bronze giants, and set them inside the palace."

sentences.zh: 」端闈，宮正門也。

translations.literal: " Duan wei—palace main gate.
translations.idiomatic: " *Duan wei* is the palace's main gate."

sentences.zh: 三輔黃圖曰：「秦宮殿端門四達，以則紫宮。

translations.literal: Three Adjuncts Yellow Chart says: "Qin palace-duan gate four reaches—to pattern Zigong.
translations.idiomatic: The *Yellow Chart of the Three Adjuncts* says: "The Qin palace's front gates opened four ways, patterned on the Purple Palace."

sentences.zh: 」仍，因也。

translations.literal: " Reng—follow.
translations.idiomatic: " *Reng* means "thereupon" or "accordingly."

sentences.zh: 衡，橫也。

translations.literal: Heng—horizontal.
translations.idiomatic: *Heng* means crosswise or horizontal.

sentences.zh: 閾，門限。

translations.literal: Yu—door sill.
translations.idiomatic: *Yu* is the doorsill or threshold.

sentences.zh: 注[五]徇猶繞也。

translations.literal: Comment [five] Xun like encircle.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [five]: *Xun* means to circle or wind around.

sentences.zh: 崇，高也。

translations.literal: Chong—high.
translations.idiomatic: *Chong* means lofty or high.

sentences.zh: 閒音閒。

translations.literal: Jian sound jian (interval).
translations.idiomatic: *Jian* is read like *jian* meaning interval or leisure.

sentences.zh: 煥，明也。

translations.literal: Huan—bright.
translations.idiomatic: *Huan* means radiant or bright.

sentences.zh: 言周回宮館，明若列星之環繞紫宮也。

translations.literal: Say circle-return palace lodges—bright like ranked stars' surround Zigong.
translations.idiomatic: This describes palaces and lodges ringing the precinct—bright as ranked stars wheeling about the Purple Palace.

sentences.zh: 環，協韻音宦。

translations.literal: Huan—rime-fit sound huan (official).
translations.idiomatic: *Huan* is read *huan* like the word for courtier to fit the verse rhyme.

sentences.zh: 注[六]三輔黃圖曰：「未央宮有清涼殿、宣室殿、中溫室殿、金華殿、大玉堂殿、中白虎殿、麒麟殿，長樂宮有神仙殿。

translations.literal: Comment [six] Three Adjuncts Yellow Chart says: "Weiyang Palace has Clear-Cool Hall, Proclamation Chamber, Central Warm-Room Hall, Brocade-Blossom Hall, Great Jade Hall, Central White-Tiger Hall, Unicorn Hall—Eternal Joy Palace has Transcendent Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [six]: The *Yellow Chart of the Three Adjuncts* lists Weiyang Palace's Clear-Cool, Proclamation Chamber, Central Warm-Room, Brocade-Blossom, Great Jade, Central White-Tiger, and Unicorn halls, and Eternal Joy Palace's Transcendent Hall.

sentences.zh: 」殫，盡也。

translations.literal: " Dan—exhaust.
translations.idiomatic: " *Dan* means "exhausted" or "entire."

sentences.zh: 注[七]增，重也。

translations.literal: Comment [seven] Zeng—layer.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [seven]: *Zeng* means layered or repeated.

sentences.zh: 盤，屈也。

translations.literal: Pan—bend.
translations.idiomatic: *Pan* means coiled or bent.

sentences.zh: 業峨，高也。

translations.literal: Ye'e—high.
translations.idiomatic: *Ye'e* describes height or towering bulk.

sentences.zh: 業音五臘反。

translations.literal: Ye sound wu la fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Ye* follows the *wu la* fanqie.

sentences.zh: 峨音我。

translations.literal: E sound wo.
translations.idiomatic: *E* is read like *wo* (I, me).

sentences.zh: 詭，異也。

translations.literal: Gui—strange.
translations.idiomatic: *Gui* means strange or uncanny.

sentences.zh: 茵，褥也。

translations.literal: Yin—mattress.
translations.idiomatic: *Yin* is a cushion or mat.

sentences.zh: 駕人曰輦。

translations.literal: Drive person called palanquin.
translations.idiomatic: A conveyance drawn by people is called a *nian* palanquin.

sentences.zh: 注[八]漢官儀曰：「婕妤以下皆居掖庭。

translations.literal: Comment [eight] Han Official Regulations says: "Below Lady of Handsome Fairness all dwell Ye Court.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [eight]: The *Han Official Regulations* says: "Every rank below Lady of Handsome Fairness lived in the Ye-court harem."

sentences.zh: 」三輔黃圖曰：「長樂宮有椒房殿。

translations.literal: " Three Adjuncts Yellow Chart says: "Eternal Joy Palace has Pepper-Room Hall.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Yellow Chart of the Three Adjuncts* notes Eternal Joy Palace's Pepper-Room Hall."

sentences.zh: 」前書曰：「班婕妤居增成捨。

translations.literal: " Former Documents says: "Lady Ban Handsome Fairness dwelt Increment-Complete Lodge.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Former Han History* says Lady Ban's quarters were called the Increment-Complete Lodge."

sentences.zh: 」桓譚新論曰：「董賢女弟為昭儀，居捨號曰椒風。

translations.literal: " Huan Tan New Treatises says: "Dong Xian's younger sister became Brilliant Companion—dwelling lodge titled Pepper-Wind.
translations.idiomatic: " Huan Tan's *New Treatises* records that Dong Xian's sister, as Brilliant Companion, lived in a lodge styled Pepper-Wind."

sentences.zh: 」漢宮閣名長安有披香殿、鴛鸞殿、飛翔殿。

translations.literal: " Han palace pavilion names Chang'an has Drape-Fragrance Hall, Mandarin-Duck Hall, Soaring Flight Hall.
translations.idiomatic: " Han palace registers list Chang'an's Drape-Fragrance, Mandarin-Duck, and Soaring Flight halls."

sentences.zh: 余未詳。

translations.literal: I not detailed.
translations.idiomatic: I have not verified the rest in detail.

sentences.zh: 注[九]昭陽殿，成帝趙昭儀所居也。

translations.literal: Comment [nine] Zhaoyang Hall—Emperor Cheng's Zhao Brilliant Companion dwelt.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [nine]: Zhaoyang Hall was the residence of Emperor Cheng's Brilliant Companion, Lady Zhao.

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「裛，纏也。

translations.literal: Shuowen says: "Yi—wrap.
translations.idiomatic: The *Shuowen* says: "*Yi* means to wrap or twine."

sentences.zh: 」音於業反。

translations.literal: " sound yu ye fan.
translations.idiomatic: " It follows the *yu ye* fanqie."

sentences.zh: 綸，糾，青絲綬也。

translations.literal: Lun, jiu—green silk ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: *Lun* and *jiu* denote green silk cords or ribbons.

sentences.zh: 「綸」或作「編」。

translations.literal: "Lun" sometimes written "bian".
translations.idiomatic: Some texts write bian instead of lun.

sentences.zh: 淮南子曰：「隨侯之珠，和氏之璧。

translations.literal: Huainanzi says: "Marquis of Sui's pearl—He Clan's jade.
translations.idiomatic: The *Huainanzi* mentions "the pearl of the Marquis of Sui and the jade disk of the house of He."

sentences.zh: 」高誘注云：「隨侯行見大蛇傷，以藥傅之。

translations.literal: " Gao You's comment says: "Marquis of Sui traveling saw great snake wounded—applied medicine daub.
translations.idiomatic: " Gao You explains that the Marquis of Sui once dressed a wounded serpent he met on the road."

sentences.zh: 後蛇銜珠以報之，因曰隨侯珠。

translations.literal: Later snake held pearl to repay—thus called Marquis of Sui pearl.
translations.idiomatic: The snake later brought him a pearl in thanks, hence the "Marquis of Sui pearl."

sentences.zh: 」說文曰：「釭，轂鐵也。

translations.literal: " Shuowen says: "Gang—hub iron.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Shuowen* defines *gang* as wheel-hub metal."

sentences.zh: 」音江，又音工。

translations.literal: " sound jiang—also sound gong.
translations.idiomatic: " Read *jiang* or *gong.*"

sentences.zh: 謂以黃金為釭，其中銜璧，納之於*

translations.literal: Means use yellow gold as gang—within bite jade disk—insert into *
translations.idiomatic: This refers to golden *gang* mounts that clamp jade disks and fit them into

translations.literal: (bi)
translations.idiomatic: (The gloss clarifies bi as "jade disk.")

sentences.zh: **[壁]*帶，為行列歷歷如錢也。

translations.literal: **[wall]* belt—make ranks distinct like coins.
translations.idiomatic: The wall bands, spaced in neat rows like coins.

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「昭陽殿璧帶，往往為黃金釭，函藍田玉璧，明珠翠羽飾之。

translations.literal: Former Documents says: "Zhaoyang Hall wall-belts—often made yellow-gold gang—encase Lantian jade disks—bright pearls kingfisher feathers adorn.
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han History* says Zhaoyang's wall bands were often capped with golden *gang* mounts enclosing Lantian jades, bright pearls, and kingfisher plumes.

sentences.zh: 」異物誌曰：「翠鳥形如燕，赤而雄曰翡，青而雌曰翠，其羽可以飾幃帳。

translations.literal: " Record of Strange Things says: "Kingfisher bird shape like swallow—red male called fei—green female called cui—its feathers can adorn curtains.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Record of Strange Things* describes kingfishers as swallow-shaped; red males are *fei*, green females *cui*, and their feathers trim curtains and bed-canopies."

sentences.zh: 」韻集曰：「火齊，珠也。

translations.literal: " Rhyme Collection says: "Huo qi—pearl.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Rhyme Collection* glosses *huo qi* as a pearl."

sentences.zh: 」戰國策曰：「應侯謂秦王曰『梁有縣黎』。

translations.literal: " Stratagems says: "Marquis of Ying told Qin King says 'Liang has county Li.'
translations.idiomatic: " The *Stratagems of the Warring States* quotes Lord Ying telling the King of Qin that Liang possessed *xuanli* jade."

sentences.zh: 」左傳曰：「晉荀息請以垂棘之璧假道於虞。

translations.literal: " Zuo Tradition says: "Jin Xun Xi requested use Chuiji's disk borrow road from Yu.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Zuo Tradition* says Jin's Xun Xi asked to borrow a path through Yu with the Chuiji jade."

sentences.zh: 」言懸黎、垂棘之玉，並夜有光輝也。

translations.literal: " Say Xuan Li, Chui Ji jades—both night have radiance.
translations.idiomatic: " Both *xuanli* and Chuiji jades glow at night."

sentences.zh: 注[一０]前書曰：「昭陽殿中庭彤朱，而殿上髹漆。

translations.literal: Comment [ten] Former Documents says: "Zhaoyang Hall central courtyard cinnabar vermilion—yet hall above lacquer paint.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [ten]: The *Former Han History* says Zhaoyang's courtyard was red with cinnabar while the hall above was lacquered black.

sentences.zh: 」髹音休。

translations.literal: " xiu sound xiu.
translations.idiomatic: " *Xiu* is read *xiu* (rest)."

sentences.zh: 漆黑故曰玄。

translations.literal: Lacquer black therefore called xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Because the lacquer was black, it is called "dark" (*xuan*).

sentences.zh: 墀，殿上地也。

translations.literal: Chi—hall above ground.
translations.idiomatic: *Chi* is the paved floor of the hall.

sentences.zh: 又曰；

translations.literal: Also says;
translations.idiomatic: The text continues:

sentences.zh: 「切皆銅沓，黃金塗，白玉階。

translations.literal: " Cut all bronze stacked—yellow gold coat—white jade steps.
translations.idiomatic: "Every joint was banded in bronze, gilded, with white-jade steps."

sentences.zh: 」扣音口。

translations.literal: " kou sound kou (mouth).
translations.idiomatic: " *Kou* is read *kou* (mouth)."

sentences.zh: □、磩，琳、□，並石次玉者。

translations.literal: [lacuna], qi—lin, [lacuna]—all stones next to jade.
translations.idiomatic: The named stones are all grades just below true jade.

sentences.zh: □音而兗反，磩音戚。

translations.literal: [lacuna] sound er yan fan—qi sound qi.
translations.idiomatic: The first character follows the *er yan* fanqie; *qi* is read like *qi* (urgency).

sentences.zh: 彩致，其文理密也。

translations.literal: Cai zhi—its grain pattern dense.
translations.idiomatic: *Cai zhi* describes how tightly figured the grain is.

sentences.zh: 青熒，其光色也。

translations.literal: Qing ying—its light color.
translations.idiomatic: *Qing ying* names the stone's greenish gleam.

sentences.zh: 漢武故事曰：「武帝起神堂，植玉樹，葺珊瑚為枝，以碧玉為葉。

translations.literal: Han Wu Tales says: "Emperor Wu raised divine hall—planted jade trees—thatch coral as branches—use green jade as leaves.
translations.idiomatic: *Han Wu Tales* says Emperor Wu built a spirit hall and set up jade trees with coral branches and green-jade leaves.

sentences.zh: 」淮南子曰：「櫫□山有碧樹在其北。

translations.literal: " Huainanzi says: "Zhu [lacuna] mountain has green tree at its north.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Huainanzi* speaks of a green tree north of Zhu[lacuna] Mountain."

sentences.zh: 」高誘注云：「碧，青石也。

translations.literal: " Gao You's comment says: "Bi—green stone.
translations.idiomatic: " Gao You glosses *bi* as bluish stone."

sentences.zh: 」謂以珠玉假為樹而植之於殿曲。

translations.literal: " Means use pearl jade false-as trees—plant them at hall's bend.
translations.idiomatic: " They fashioned pearl and jade into artificial trees and set them in the recesses of the hall."

sentences.zh: 阿，曲也。

translations.literal: E—bend.
translations.idiomatic: *E* means a bend or niche.

sentences.zh: 注[一一]薛綜注西京賦曰：「颯纚，長袖貌。

translations.literal: Comment [eleven] Xue Zong's note on Western Capital Rhapsody says: "Sa li—long sleeve appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [eleven]: Xue Zong's gloss on the *Western Capital Rhapsody* says *sa li* depicts sweeping sleeves.

sentences.zh: 颯音素合反，纚音山綺反。

translations.literal: Sa sound su he fan—li sound shan qi fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Sa* follows the *su he* fanqie; *li* follows *shan qi*.

sentences.zh: 」綺，文繒也。

translations.literal: " qi—patterned silk.
translations.idiomatic: " *Qi* is figured silk."

sentences.zh: 組，綬也。

translations.literal: Zu—ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: *Zu* refers to woven ribbons or cords.

sentences.zh: 繽紛，盛魍。

translations.literal: Bin fen—abundant wang.
translations.idiomatic: *Bin fen* means riotously abundant ornament.

sentences.zh: 燭，照也。

translations.literal: Zhu—shine.
translations.idiomatic: *Zhu* means to illuminate.

sentences.zh: 言精彩華飾照耀也。

translations.literal: Say brilliant splendor splendid adornments shine.
translations.idiomatic: This describes dazzling finery lit up in brilliance.

sentences.zh: 戰國策張儀謂秦王曰：「彼周、鄭之女，粉白黛黑立於衢，非知而見之者以為神也。」

translations.literal: Stratagems Zhang Yi told Qin King says: "Those Zhou, Zheng women—powder white eyebrow black stand at crossroads—not know yet see them—think spirits.
translations.idiomatic: In the *Stratagems*, Zhang Yi tells the King of Qin that Zhou and Zheng girls, powdered and kohled, standing at crossroads seemed divine to passersby who did not know better.

sentences.zh: 注[一二]前書曰：「漢興，因秦之稱號，正嫡稱皇后，妾皆稱夫人。

translations.literal: Comment [twelve] Former Documents says: "Han rose—inherited Qin's titles—proper heir called empress—concubines all called ladies.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [twelve]: The *Former Han History* says that at the dynasty's founding Han kept Qin's nomenclature: the chief consort was empress and lesser wives were ladies.

sentences.zh: 凡十四等，有昭儀、婕妤、娙娥、傛華、美人、八子、充衣、七子、良人、長使、少使、五官、順常，是為十三等；

translations.literal: In all fourteen ranks—have Brilliant Companion, Lady of Handsome Fairness, Lady of Beauty, Lady of Radiance, Beauty, Eighth-Rank Son, Charged Dress, Seventh-Rank Son, Fair Woman, Elder Envoy, Junior Envoy, Five Offices, Ever Constant—these are thirteen ranks;
translations.idiomatic: There were nominally fourteen ranks: Brilliant Companion down through Ever Constant count as thirteen;

sentences.zh: 又有無涓、共和、娛靈、保林、良使、夜者，秩祿同，共為一等，合十四位也。

translations.literal: Also have No Impurity, Shared Harmony, Joyful Spirit, Protected Grove, Fair Envoy, Night Watch—salary same—together form one rank—combine fourteen positions.
translations.idiomatic: No Impurity, Shared Harmony, Joyful Spirit, Protected Grove, Fair Envoy, and Night Watch shared pay and counted together as one rank, making fourteen posts in all.

sentences.zh: 」窈窕，幽閒也。

translations.literal: " Yao tiao—quiet serene.
translations.idiomatic: " *Yao tiao* means secluded grace."

sentences.zh: 繁華，美麗也。

translations.literal: Fan hua—beautiful splendid.
translations.idiomatic: *Fan hua* means radiant beauty.

sentences.zh: 百數謂以百而數之也。

translations.literal: Hundred count—means by hundred count them.
translations.idiomatic: "Numbered in the hundreds" means reckoned by hundreds.

sentences.zh: 注[一三]蕭何、曹參並沛人，魏相字弱翁，濟陰人，邴吉字少卿，魯國人，並為丞相。

translations.literal: Comment [thirteen] Xiao He, Cao Shen both Pei men—Wei Xiang courtesy Weak Elder Jiyin man—Bing Ji courtesy Junior Minister Lu nation men—all became chancellors.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [thirteen]: Xiao He and Cao Shen were both from Pei; Wei Xiang (Weak Elder) came from Jiyin; Bing Ji (Junior Minister) from Lu—and they all rose to chancellor.

sentences.zh: 注[一四]李陵書曰：「其餘佐命立功之士。

translations.literal: Comment [fourteen] Li Ling's letter says: "The rest assist mandate merit-establish knights.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [fourteen]: Li Ling's letter speaks of "those who aided the dynastic mandate and won merit."

sentences.zh: 」司馬相如曰：「垂統理順易繼也。

translations.literal: " Sima Xiangru says: "Hang lineage principle smooth easy succeed.
translations.idiomatic: " Sima Xiangru says a smooth succession hangs on orderly transmission of the lineage."

sentences.zh: 」統，業也。

translations.literal: " tong—enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: " *Tong* means the inherited enterprise."

sentences.zh: 禮記曰：「保者慎其身以輔翼之。

translations.literal: Record of Rites says: "Protectors carefully their persons to assist wings.
translations.idiomatic: The *Record of Rites* says guardians watch themselves closely while supporting the ruler.

sentences.zh: 」愷，樂也。

translations.literal: " kai—joy.
translations.idiomatic: " *Kai* means joyful kindness."

sentences.zh: 悌，易也。

translations.literal: Ti—easy.
translations.idiomatic: *Ti* here means easygoing gentleness.

sentences.zh: 楊雄長楊賦曰：「今朝廷出愷悌，行簡易。

translations.literal: Yang Xiong Tall Poplar Rhapsody says: "Now court puts forth kai ti—practice simple easy.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xiong's *Tall Poplar Rhapsody* says the court now shows kindness and follows plain, easy rule.

sentences.zh: 」王□四子講德論曰：「秦之處位任政者，並施毒螫。

translations.literal: " Wang [lacuna] Four Masters Discussing Virtue says: "Qin's occupying positions wielding policy—all applied poison sting.
translations.idiomatic: " Wang [lacuna]'s *Four Masters Discussing Virtue* says Qin's officials ruled with venomous cruelty."

sentences.zh: 」前書曰：「孝惠、高後之時，海內得離戰國之苦，君臣俱欲無為，而天下晏然，衣食滋殖。

translations.literal: " Former Documents says: "Emperor Hui, Empress Gao times—within seas obtained leave Warring States bitterness—lord minister both desire non-action—yet realm tranquil—clothes food multiply breed.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Former Han History* says that under Emperor Hui and Empress Gao the realm escaped Warring States misery; rulers and ministers favored quiet rule, the land grew calm, and livelihoods flourished."

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「近觀漢相，高祖開基，蕭、曹為冠。

translations.literal: " Also says: "Near observe Han chancellors—Gaozu opened foundation—Xiao, Cao as crown.
translations.idiomatic: " It adds that among Han chancellors since Gaozu's founding Xiao He and Cao Shen stood first."

sentences.zh: 孝宣中興，丙、魏有聲。

translations.literal: Emperor Xuan middle revival—Bing, Wei have fame.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan's revival brought Bing and Wei renown.

sentences.zh: 」是時黜陟有序，觿職修理，公卿多稱其位，海內興於禮讓也。

translations.literal: " This time demote promote have order—horn duties repair manage—dukes ministers mostly fit their posts—within seas rise at ritual yielding.
translations.idiomatic: " Appointments followed merit, offices ran true, nobles suited their stations, and the realm turned to courtesy and deference."

sentences.zh: 注[一六]三輔故事曰：「天祿、石渠並閣名，在未央宮北，以閣秘書。

translations.literal: Comment [sixteen] Three Adjuncts Tales says: "Heaven's Blessing, Stone Canal both pavilion names—at Weiyang Palace north—use pavilion secret books.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [sixteen]: The *Three Adjuncts Tales* names Heaven's Blessing and Stone Canal towers north of Weiyang where palace books were stored.

sentences.zh: 」諄誨謂殷勤教告也。

translations.literal: " Zhun hui means earnest teach inform.
translations.idiomatic: " *Zhun hui* means tirelessly instructing."

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「誨爾諄諄。

translations.literal: Odes Greater Elegant says: "Instruct you earnest-earnest.
translations.idiomatic: The *Greater Odes* says "I instruct you again and again."

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「我教告王，口語諄諄然。

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan comment says: "I teach inform king—mouth words earnest-earnest thus.
translations.idiomatic: " Zheng Xuan explains that the speaker admonishes the king with repeated oral counsel."

sentences.zh: 」諄音之純反。

translations.literal: " zhun sound zhi chun fan.
translations.idiomatic: " *Zhun* follows the *zhi chun* fanqie."

sentences.zh: 六蓺謂詩、書、禮、樂、易、春秋也。

translations.literal: Six arts mean Odes, Documents, Rites, Music, Changes, Spring-Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: The six arts are the Odes, Documents, Rites, Music, Changes, and Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 稽，考也。

translations.literal: Ji—examine.
translations.idiomatic: *Ji* means to investigate or collate.

sentences.zh: 前書，甘露中詔諸儒講五經同異，令蕭望之平奏其議。

translations.literal: Former Documents—Ganlu middle edict various scholars lecture Five Classics same different—order Xiao Wangzhi level memorialize their deliberations.
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han History* records that under Ganlu an edict summoned scholars to debate the Five Classics and ordered Xiao Wangzhi to summarize their findings.

sentences.zh: 注[一七]承明，殿前之廬也。

translations.literal: Comment [seventeen] Chengming—hall front's hut.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [seventeen]: Chengming was the lodge before the hall.

sentences.zh: 金馬，署名也。

translations.literal: Golden Horse—office title.
translations.idiomatic: Golden Horse names an official bureau.

sentences.zh: 門有銅馬，故名金馬門，待詔者皆居之。

translations.literal: Gate has bronze horse—thus named Golden Horse Gate—await edict ones all dwell.
translations.idiomatic: A bronze horse stood by the gate, hence Golden Horse Gate, where expectant scholars lodged.

sentences.zh: 宏亦大也。

translations.literal: Hong also great.
translations.idiomatic: *Hong* likewise means vast.

sentences.zh: 元其元，本其本。

translations.literal: Prime its prime—root its root.
translations.idiomatic: It traces each topic to first principles.

sentences.zh: 秘文，秘書也。

translations.literal: Secret writing—secret documents.
translations.idiomatic: *Mi wen* refers to palace archives.

sentences.zh: 孝經鉤命決曰「丘掇秘文」也。

translations.literal: Classic of Filial Piety Hooking Fate Judgment says "Qiu gather secret writing".
translations.idiomatic: The *Hooking Fate Judgment* on the *Classic of Filial Piety* says Kong Qiu "gathered secret texts."

sentences.zh: 注[一八]周，環也。

translations.literal: Comment [eighteen] Zhou—circle.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [eighteen]: *Zhou* means to ring or encircle.

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「鉤陳，紫宮外星也，宮□之位亦象之。

translations.literal: Former Documents Sounds Meanings says: "Hook Array—Purple Palace outer stars—palace [lacuna] positions also image.
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han Sounds and Meanings* says Hook Array is outside the Purple Palace and palace stations mirror it.

sentences.zh: 」嚴更之署，行夜之司也。

translations.literal: " Strict Watch's office—night patrol's bureau.
translations.idiomatic: " These are offices for night watches and patrol duty."

sentences.zh: 禮官，奉常也，有博士掌試策，考其優劣，為甲乙之科，即前書曰「太常以公孫弘為下第」是也。

translations.literal: Rites officer—Grand Upholder—have erudites grasp trial essays—examine their excellence inferior—make jia yi ranks—namely Former Documents says "Grand Upholder took Gongsun Hong as lower grade" is this.
translations.idiomatic: The Minister of Rites (Grand Upholder) oversaw examination essays ranked from top to bottom, as when the *Former Han History* notes Gongsun Hong placed in the lowest tier.

sentences.zh: 言百郡，舉全數。

translations.literal: Say hundred commanderies—raise whole count.
translations.idiomatic: "The hundred commanderies" states the full tally rhetorically.

sentences.zh: 前書又曰：「興廉舉孝。」

translations.literal: Former Documents also says: "Raise incorrupt recommend filial."
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han History* also speaks of promoting the honest and filial.

sentences.zh: 注[一九]虎賁，宿□之臣。

translations.literal: Comment [nineteen] Tiger Runner—night [lacuna] ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [nineteen]: Tiger Runners are household guards.

sentences.zh: 贅衣，主衣之官。

translations.literal: Surplus clothes—master clothes officers.
translations.idiomatic: *Zhui yi* officers oversaw wardrobe.

sentences.zh: 贅，綴也，音之銳反。

translations.literal: Zhui—attach—sound zhi rui fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Zhui* means to tie or attach; it follows the *zhi rui* fanqie.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「綴衣虎賁。

translations.literal: Shang Documents says: "Attach clothes tiger runners.
translations.idiomatic: The *Scriptures* pairs wardrobe stewards with Tiger Runners.

sentences.zh: 」閹尹、閽寺並宦官，周禮有閹人、寺人。

translations.literal: " Eunuch directors, gatekeepers both palace officers—Zhou Rites have castrate men, temple men.
translations.idiomatic: " Eunuch directors and gatekeepers were palace staff like the Zhou Rites' castrates and temple attendants."

sentences.zh: 陛戟，執戟於陛也。

translations.literal: Terrace halberds—hold halberds at terrace.
translations.idiomatic: Terrace halberdiers stood guard on the steps.

sentences.zh: 百重，言多也。

translations.literal: Hundred layers—say many.
translations.idiomatic: "A hundredfold" means simply "many."

sentences.zh: 攸，所也。

translations.literal: You—place.
translations.idiomatic: *You* means "where" or "the place that."

sentences.zh: 司，主也，協韻音伺。

translations.literal: Si—direct—rime-fit sound si (watch).

translations.idiomatic: Comment [twenty-two]: Weiyang lay west, Eternal Joy east, Cassia and Radiant Light north—linked by flying galleries.

translations.idiomatic: *Yong* means the city wall.

translations.literal: Hun—same.
translations.idiomatic: *Hun* means merged or identical.

translations.literal: Establish Splendor Palace at city west.
translations.idiomatic: Establish Splendor Palace stood west of the city.

translations.literal: Shu—connect.
translations.idiomatic: *Shu* means joined or continuous.

translations.literal: (gate)
translations.idiomatic: (The gloss supplies the word "gate.")

translations.idiomatic: It rose over twenty zhang tall; to the south stood jade gates and similar structures.

translations.idiomatic: " *Gu* sounds like *gu* (orphan); *leng* follows *li deng*."

sentences.zh: 其上棲金雀焉。

translations.literal: Its above perch golden sparrow.
translations.idiomatic: Golden sparrows perched atop them.

translations.idiomatic: The *Three Adjuncts Tales* mentions bronze phoenixes on Establish Splendor's towers—the "golden sparrows."

sentences.zh: 注[二三]三輔故事曰：「建章宮東有折風闕。

translations.idiomatic: Comment [twenty-three]: The *Three Adjuncts Tales* places the Bend Wind tower east of Establish Splendor.

sentences.zh: 」關中記曰：「折風一名別風。

translations.literal: " Within Pass Record says: "Bend Wind one name Separate Wind.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Record of the Pass* calls Bend Wind also Separate Wind."

sentences.zh: 」嶕嶢，高也。

translations.literal: " jiao yao—high.
translations.idiomatic: " *Jiao yao* means towering."

translations.literal: Jiao sound jiao—yao sound yao.
translations.idiomatic: *Jiao* is read *jiao*; *yao* follows the usual reading for the lofty graph.

sentences.zh: 前書曰，建章宮度為千門萬戶。

translations.literal: Former Documents says—Establish Splendor Palace measure as thousand gates myriad doors.
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han History* says Establish Splendor spanned a thousand gates and myriad doors.

sentences.zh: 合謂之陰，開謂之陽。

translations.literal: Shut called yin—open called yang.
translations.idiomatic: Closed panels count as yin; open ones as yang.

sentences.zh: 易曰：「闔戶謂之坤，辟戶謂之干。」

translations.literal: Changes says: "Shut door called kun—open door called qian."
translations.idiomatic: The *Changes* says shutting the door is kun and opening it is qian.

sentences.zh: 注[二四]正殿即前殿也。

translations.literal: Comment [twenty-four] Main hall namely front hall.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [twenty-four]: The main hall is the front hall.

sentences.zh: 層，重也。

translations.literal: Ceng—layer.
translations.idiomatic: *Ceng* means stacked stories.

sentences.zh: 臨乎未央，言高之極也。

translations.literal: Overlook unto Weiyang—say high's utmost.
translations.idiomatic: "Overlooking Weiyang" stresses its extraordinary height.

sentences.zh: 關中記建章宮有駘蕩、馺娑、枍詣殿。

translations.literal: Within Pass Record Establish Splendor Palace has Idle Sweep, Swift Glide, Catalpa Reach halls.
translations.idiomatic: *Record of the Pass* lists Establish Splendor's Idle Sweep, Swift Glide, and Catalpa Reach halls.

sentences.zh: 天梁亦宮名也。

translations.literal: Heaven Beam also palace name.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven Beam is another palace name.

sentences.zh: 駘音殆，蕩音蕩。

translations.literal: Dai sound dai—dang sound dang.
translations.idiomatic: *Dai* follows the *dai* fanqie; *dang* as in "to sweep."

sentences.zh: 馺音素合反，娑音素可反。

translations.literal: Sa sound su he fan—suo sound su ke fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Sa* follows the *su he* fanqie; *suo* follows *su ke*.

sentences.zh: 枍音烏計反。

translations.literal: Yi sound wu ji fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Yi* follows the *wu ji* fanqie.

sentences.zh: 小雅曰：「蓋戴，覆也。

translations.literal: Minor Odes says: "Gai dai—cover.
translations.idiomatic: The *Minor Odes* glosses *gai dai* as "to cover over."

sentences.zh: 」反宇謂飛簷上反也。

translations.literal: " Fan yu means flying eaves upward turn.
translations.idiomatic: " *Fan yu* names the upturned sweep of the flying eaves."

sentences.zh: 激日謂日影激入於殿內也。

translations.literal: Shot sun means sun shadow shoots enter hall inside.
translations.idiomatic: "Shot sunlight" describes rays striking deep into the hall.

sentences.zh: 注[二五]神明，台名也。

translations.literal: Comment [twenty-five] Divine Bright—terrace name.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [twenty-five]: Divine Bright was the name of a terrace.

sentences.zh: 躋，升也。

translations.literal: Ji—ascend.
translations.idiomatic: *Ji* means to climb.

sentences.zh: 偃蹇，高貌也。

translations.literal: Yan jian—high appearance.
translations.idiomatic: *Yan jian* describes lofty loftiness.

sentences.zh: 軼，過也。

translations.literal: Yi—surpass.
translations.idiomatic: *Yi* means to overshoot or pass beyond.

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「凡數三分有二為太半。

translations.literal: Former Documents Sounds Meanings says: "All fractions three parts two parts—called great half.
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han Sounds and Meanings* defines "greater half" as two of three parts.

sentences.zh: 」說文曰：「棼，棟也。

translations.literal: " Shuowen says: "Fen—ridge-beam.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Shuowen* glosses *fen* as the ridge-beam."

sentences.zh: 」爾雅曰：「楣謂之梁。

translations.literal: " Erya says: "Mei called beam.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Erya* calls the lintel *mei*."

sentences.zh: 」郭璞云：「門戶上橫樑也。

translations.literal: " Guo Pu says: "Door gate upper crossbeam.
translations.idiomatic: " Guo Pu explains it as the crossbeam above the doorway."

sentences.zh: 」方言曰：「僄，輕也。

translations.literal: " Dialects says: "Piao—light.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Dialects* glosses *piao* as "light" or "fleet."

sentences.zh: 」音匹妙反。

translations.literal: " sound pi miao fan.
translations.idiomatic: " It follows the *pi miao* fanqie."
