translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan's *Record of Rites* note glosses *jiao* as swift.

sentences.zh: 」字書曰：「愕，驚也。

translations.literal: " Character Book says: "E—startle.

translations.idiomatic: " It follows the *wu ge* fanqie."

translations.idiomatic: The *Character Forest* defines *yi* as a stunned look.

sentences.zh: 」音丑吏反。

translations.literal: " sound chou li fan.
translations.idiomatic: " It follows the *chou li* fanqie."

sentences.zh: 注[二六]井干，樓名也。

translations.literal: Comment [twenty-six] Well-Crib—tower name.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [twenty-six]: Well-Crib was the name of a tower.

translations.idiomatic: " *Liezi* lists five fairy peaks in the sea: Dai Yu, Yuan Qiao, Fang Hu, Yingzhou, and Penglai."

sentences.zh: 注[三０]靈草、神本謂不死藥也。

translations.idiomatic: Comment [thirty]: Spirit grasses and divine stems mean elixirs of immortality.

translations.idiomatic: The *Records* says the sea peaks hold the immortals' deathless drugs.

sentences.zh: 」崢嶸，高峻也。

translations.literal: " Zheng rong—high steep.
translations.idiomatic: " *Zheng rong* means towering crags."

translations.idiomatic: *Zheng* follows *shi geng*; *rong* like *hong* (vast).

translations.idiomatic: Comment [thirty-one]: The *Former Han History* records Emperor Wu's bronze pillars, dew-catching palms, and similar works.

sentences.zh: 三輔故事云：「建章宮承露盤，高二十丈，大七圍，以銅為之。

translations.idiomatic: The *Three Adjuncts Tales* describes Establish Splendor's dew basin—twenty zhang tall, seven girths around, cast in bronze.

sentences.zh: 上有仙人掌承露，和玉屑飲之。

translations.literal: Above has immortal palm catch dew—mix jade crumbs drink.
translations.idiomatic: Immortal palms caught the dew to mix with powdered jade for drinking.

sentences.zh: 」金莖即銅柱也。

translations.literal: " Golden stems namely bronze pillars.
translations.idiomatic: " The golden stems are the bronze pillars."

sentences.zh: 軼，過也。

translations.literal: Yi—surpass.
translations.idiomatic: *Yi* means to rise beyond.

translations.literal: Ai ai—dust.
translations.idiomatic: *Ai ai* is airborne dust and haze.

sentences.zh: 鮮，絜也。

translations.literal: Xian—pure.
translations.idiomatic: *Xian* means pure or bright-clean.

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「顥，白魍。

translations.literal: Shuowen says: "Hao—white wang.
translations.idiomatic: The *Shuowen* glosses *hao* as radiant white.

sentences.zh: 」音皓。

translations.literal: " sound hao.
translations.idiomatic: " Read *hao* (bright)."

sentences.zh: 注[三二]丕，大也。

translations.literal: Comment [thirty-two] Pi—great.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [thirty-two]: *Pi* means grand.

sentences.zh: 誕，欺也。

translations.literal: Dan—deceive.
translations.idiomatic: *Dan* means boastful deceit.

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「齊人李少翁以方士見上，上拜為文成將軍，言於上曰：『即欲與神通，宮室被服非象神，神物不至。

translations.literal: Former Documents says: "Qi man Li Shao Weng by recipe-master saw emperor—emperor invested General Wencheng—spoke to emperor says: 'If wish spirit-connect—palace chambers clothes not image spirits—spirit things not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han History* says the Qi fangshi Li Shaoweng became General Wencheng and told the emperor that without spirit-like halls and robes the gods would not come.

sentences.zh: 』乃作甘泉宮，中為台，畫天、地、泰一諸鬼神，而置祭具以致天神。

translations.literal: " Then made Sweet Springs Palace—inside made terrace—paint Heaven, Earth, Grand Unity various ghosts gods—then place sacrifice tools to summon heaven spirits.
translations.idiomatic: " He built Sweet Springs Palace with terraces painted with Heaven, Earth, Grand Unity, and spirits, and set out offerings to draw down celestial powers."

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「膠東人欒大多方略而敢為大言，言曰：『臣常往東海中，見安期、羨門之屬。

translations.literal: " Also says: "Jiaodong man Luan Da much stratagem dare great boasts—says: 'Your servant often went east sea inside—saw Anqi, Xianmen kinds.
translations.idiomatic: " It adds that Jiaodong's Luan Da was bold and boastful, claiming he had met immortals such as Anqi Sheng and Xianmen Gao in the eastern sea."

sentences.zh: 』乃拜為五利將軍。

translations.literal: " Then invested Five Profits General.
translations.idiomatic: " He was therefore named Five Profits general."

sentences.zh: 」刑，法也。

translations.literal: " xing—law.
translations.idiomatic: " *Xing* means punishment under law."

sentences.zh: 列仙傳曰：「赤松子者，神農時雨師也，服水玉以教神農。

translations.literal: Arrayed Transcendents Record says: "Red Pine Master—Shennong times rain master—ate water jade to teach Shennong.
translations.idiomatic: The *Arrayed Transcendents* names Red Pine Master as Shennong's rain master who taught him after eating "water jade."

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「王子喬者，周靈王太子晉，道士浮丘公接以上嵩山。」

translations.literal: " Also says: "Prince Wang Qiao—King Ling of Zhou heir Jin—Daoist Fuqiu Duke lifted above Song Mountain."
translations.idiomatic: " It also tells how Daoist Fuqiu Gong lifted Prince Jin of Zhou to Mount Song."

sentences.zh: 爾乃盛娛游之壯觀，奮大武乎上囿，因茲以威戎誇狄，耀威而講事。

translations.literal: Then flourish amusement roam grand sight—raise great martial at upper park—by this awe Rong boast Di—flash might lecture affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Then came the grand spectacle of royal outings and martial display in the upper hunting park—thereby awing the Rong, vaunting over the Di, brandishing might while holding the martial review.

sentences.zh: [一]命荊州使起鳥，詔梁野而驅獸，毛腢內闐，飛羽上覆，接翼側足，集禁林而屯聚。

translations.literal: [One] Order Jingzhou dispatch raise birds—edict Liang wilds drive beasts—fur tribes inner fill—fly feathers above cover—meet wings side feet—gather forbidden forest mass.
translations.idiomatic: [One] They ordered Jingzhou to flush game birds and summoned the Liang wilderness to drive beasts—fur-clad creatures thronged below while wings darkened the sky above, birds packing wing to wing and talon beside talon, crowding the imperial grove.

sentences.zh: [二] 水衡虞人，理其營表，種別腢分，部曲有署。

translations.literal: [Two] Water Superintendent foresters—manage their camp markers—species divide tribe divide—companies have stations.
translations.idiomatic: [Two] The Water Superintendent's foresters set camp boundaries, sorted quarry by species and ground, and assigned each unit its station.

sentences.zh: [三]罘罔連紘，籠山絡野，列卒周□，星羅雲布。

translations.literal: [Three] Snare nets join cords—cage hills rope plains—array soldiers surround [lacuna]—stars spread clouds deploy.
translations.idiomatic: [Three] Nets and cords linked roofed the hills and spanned the wilds; ranks closed the ring—spread like stars, arrayed like clouds.

sentences.zh: [四]於是乘*

translations.literal: [Four] Then mount *
translations.idiomatic: [Four] Then he mounted the bell-decked

translations.literal: (bells)
translations.idiomatic: (The gloss supplies "bells" of the state carriage.)

sentences.zh: *輿備法駕，帥腢臣，披飛廉，入苑門。

translations.literal: * carriage prepare law escort—lead host ministers—breast Flying Gale—enter park gate.
translations.idiomatic: He took the bell-decked carriage in full formal escort, led every minister past Flying Gale Gate, and entered the park.

sentences.zh: [五] 遂繞酆鎬，歷上蘭，六師發冑，百獸駭殫，震震爚爚，雷奔電激，草木塗地，山淵反覆，蹂蹸其十二三，乃拗怒而少息。

translations.literal: [Five] Then circle Feng Hao—pass Upper Orchid—six armies loose helmets—hundred beasts alarm exhausted—quake quake blaze blaze—thunder run lightning whip—grass trees smear earth—mountains pools overturn—trample twelve three—then twisted wrath slight rest.
translations.idiomatic: [Five] They swept past Feng and Hao, crossed Upper Orchid Terrace; the six hosts threw off helmet straps as beasts scattered and fell—earth shook and light flashed like thunderbolts, flora flattened and hillsides overturned until a tenth or more were trampled; only then did fury slacken.

sentences.zh: [六]爾乃期門佽飛，列刃鑽鍭，要趹追蹤，鳥驚觸絲，獸駭值鋒，機不□掎，弦不再控，矢無單殺，中必疊雙，颮颮紛紛，矰繳相纏，風毛雨血，灑野蔽天。

translations.literal: [Six] Then Gate Guards Flying Archers—array blades bore broadheads—intercept leap chase tracks—birds alarm touch silk—beasts fright meet edge—trigger not [lacuna] drag—string not again draw—arrow no single kill—hit must pile pair—whirl whirl riot riot—bird bolts cords twine—wind fur rain blood—spray wild screen sky.
translations.idiomatic: [Six] Gate Guards and flying archers drew blades and broadheads, cutting off every escape—birds struck the nets, beasts ran onto spear-points; triggers snapped without pause, bowstrings thrummed without cease; no arrow claimed one quarry alone, each shot piled kills two deep—shafts whistled, cords tangled, fur flew like wind and blood fell like rain until field and sky went dark.

sentences.zh: [七]平原赤，勇士厲，譸狖失木，豺狼攝竄。

translations.literal: [Seven] Plain red—brave warriors fierce—long-arm gibbons lose trees—wolves jackals cower flee.
translations.idiomatic: [Seven] The flats ran red and warriors blazed with zeal; long-armed apes fled the woods and wolves slunk away in terror.

sentences.zh: [八]爾乃移師趨險，並蹈潛穢，窮虎奔突，狂兕觸□。

translations.literal: [Eight] Then shift hosts hurry peril—together tread hidden foul—exhausted tiger rush thrust—mad rhino strike [lacuna].
translations.idiomatic: [Eight] The columns turned into rough ground and plunged through tangled thickets—cornered tigers charged and maddened rhinos crashed [lacuna].

sentences.zh: [九]許少施巧，秦成力折，掎僄狡，診猛噬，脫角挫脰，徒搏獨殺。

translations.literal: [Nine] Xu Shao apply skill—Qin Cheng strength break—drag fleet sly—gauge fierce bite—strip horn break neck—bare wrestle alone kill.
translations.idiomatic: [Nine] Xu Shao fought with craft and Qin Cheng with brute strength—they tripped fleet quarry, seized savage biters, snapped horns and necks, and brought down beasts bare-handed, each hunter claiming his kill alone.

sentences.zh: [一０]挾師豹，拖熊螭，頓犀犛，曳豪羆，超迥壑，越峻崖，□巉巖，鉅石隤，松□僕，叢林摧，草木無餘，禽獸殄夷。

translations.literal: [Ten] Hug master leopard—drag bear chi—halt rhino yak—pull hair bear—leap far gorge—cross steep cliff—[lacuna] jagged crags—great stone fall—pine [lacuna] fall—thicket forest smash—grass trees none remainder—birds beasts exterminate level.
translations.idiomatic: [Ten] They grappled leopards, dragged bears and hornless dragons, wrestled rhinos and yaks, hauled giant bears across chasms and cliffs—scree gave way, boulders tumbled, pines crashed, brush shattered; not a blade of grass remained and every beast was wiped out.

sentences.zh: [一一]於是天子乃登屬玉之館，歷長楊之榭，覽山川之體埶，觀三軍之殺獲，原野蕭條，目極四裔，禽相鎮厭，獸相枕藉。

translations.literal: [Eleven] Then Son of Heaven mounts Jade Cockatrice lodge—pass Tall Poplar kiosk—view mountains rivers body terrain—watch three armies kill capture—wild plain bleak—eye reach four frontiers—birds mutually press weary—beasts mutually pillow mat.
translations.idiomatic: [Eleven] The emperor climbed the Jade Cockatrice lodge and the Tall Poplar terrace, scanned landforms and watched the hosts tally kills—the wasteland lay bare to the horizon's edge, birds piled in heaps and beasts sprawled flank on flank.

sentences.zh: [一二]然後收禽會觿，論功賜胙，陳輕騎以行炰，騰酒車而斟酌，割鮮野食，舉燧命爵。

translations.literal: [Twelve] Then gather birds assemble horn-knives—debate merit grant roast meat—array light riders carry roast—raise wine cart pour pour—cut fresh wild eat—lift torch command cups.
translations.idiomatic: [Twelve] Then they gathered quarry and horn knives, ranked merit and handed out sacrificial cuts—light horse swept in roasts, wine carts rolled up for pouring—they carved fresh meat in the field, lit torches, and passed the cups by rank.

sentences.zh: [一三] 饗賜畢，勞逸齊，大輅鳴鸞，容與□回，集乎豫章之宇，臨乎昆明之池。

translations.literal: [Thirteen] Feast grant finish—labor rest equal—great carriage bell orioles—ease ease [lacuna] turn—gather Yu Zhang hall—overlook Kunming pool.
translations.idiomatic: [Thirteen] When feasting ended and weariness faded alike, the great bell-carriage rolled at ease, circling slowly toward Yu Zhang Hall and halting above Kunming Pool.

sentences.zh: [一四] 左牽牛而右織女，似雲漢之無崖，茂樹蔭蔚，芳草被堤，蘭□髮色，曄曄猗猗，若摛錦布繡，燭耀乎其陂。

translations.literal: [Fourteen] Left Lead Ox right Weaver Maid—like Cloud River without cliff—lush trees shade lush—fragrant grass cover bank—orchid [lacuna] bloom color—blaze blaze lush lush—like spread brocade lay embroidery—torch dazzle its slope.
translations.idiomatic: [Fourteen] Oxherd stood left and Weaver right like figures along the endless Milky Way—thick trees shaded the banks, sweet herbs clothed the dikes, orchids flashed color, gleaming and lush as spread brocade, torch-bright across the slope.

sentences.zh: [一五]玄鶴白鷺，黃鵠鵁鸛，鶬鴰鴇鶂，鳧鷖鴻鴈，朝發河海，夕宿江漢，沉浮往來，雲集霧散。

translations.literal: [Fifteen] Dark crane white egret—yellow swan crested stork—oriole magpie bustard grebe—duck gull wild goose wild goose—morning depart river sea—evening lodge Yangtze Han—float sink come go—cloud gather mist scatter.
translations.idiomatic: [Fifteen] Dark cranes and egrets, swans and herons, orioles and grebes, ducks, gulls, and wild geese—morning flights from river and sea, evening roosts on Yangtze and Han—wheeling aloft and settling again, thick as clouds then gone like mist.

sentences.zh: [一六]於是後宮乘輚路，登龍舟，張鳳蓋，建華旗，袪黼帷，鏡清流，靡微風，澹淡浮。

translations.literal: [Sixteen] Then rear palace ride screened carriage—mount dragon boat—spread phoenix canopy—raise flower banner—lift brocade curtain—mirror clear flow—scatter slight wind—ripple ripple float.
translations.idiomatic: [Sixteen] Harem ladies boarded screened coaches and dragon barges, raised phoenix parasols and silken banners, drew aside embroidered curtains, mirrored themselves in clear currents, and drifted gently where breezes barely stirred the waves.

sentences.zh: [一七]棹女謳，鼓吹震，聲激越，謍厲天，鳥腢翔，魚窺淵。

translations.literal: [Seventeen] Oar maidens sing—drums pipes shake—sound piercing—noise harsh heaven—birds tribes soar—fish peep depths.
translations.idiomatic: [Seventeen] Maidens at the oars sang while drums and pipes shook the air—the notes pierced skyward; birds wheeled overhead and fish stared up from the depths.

sentences.zh: [一八]招白閒，下雙鵠，揄文竿，出比目。

translations.literal: [Eighteen] Summon white leisure—lower twin swans—lift patterned rods—bring out flatfish.
translations.idiomatic: [Eighteen] They lured whitefish and paired swans, flicked patterned rods, and drew flatfish from the deep.

sentences.zh: [一九]撫鴻幢，御矰繳，方舟並騖，俛仰極樂。

translations.literal: [Nineteen] Stroke great banners—manage bird bolts—paired boats race together—bow rise extreme joy.
translations.idiomatic: [Nineteen] They skimmed broad pennants, worked fowling arrows, raced paired boats side by side, and bent and lifted with utter delight.

sentences.zh: [二０]遂風舉雲搖，浮游普覽，前乘秦領，後越九嵕，[二一]東薄河華，西涉岐雍，宮館所歷，百有餘區，行所朝夕，儲不改供。

translations.literal: [Twenty] Then wind lift cloud sway—float roam universal view—front ride Qin Ridge—rear leap Nine Peaks—[Twenty-one] east press Yellow River Mount Hua—west ford Qi Yong—palace lodges passed—hundred have surplus districts—travel place dawn dusk—stores not change supply.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty] Wind-borne and cloud-swung they roamed far—beyond Qin Ridge and past the Nine Peaks, east to the Yellow River and Mount Hua, west across Qi and Yong—more than a hundred palace lodges lined the route, yet dawn-to-dusk travel never outran the granaries.

sentences.zh: [二二]禮上下而接山川，究休佑之所用，采游童之歡謠，第從臣之嘉頌。

translations.literal: [Twenty-two] Rites upper lower meet mountains rivers—plumb blessing aid place use—gather roaming children's joy songs—rank attendant ministers fine hymns.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-two] They observed ritual above and below and welcomed spirits of hill and stream, sounding out omens of blessing; they gathered children's songs from the roads and ranked ministers' celebratory odes.

sentences.zh: [二三]於斯之時，都都相望，邑邑相屬，國藉十世之基，家承百年之業，士食舊德之名氏，農服先疇之畎畝，商修族世之所鬻，工用高曾之規矩，粲乎隱隱，各得其所。

translations.literal: [Twenty-three] At this time—capitals mutual gaze—districts mutual link—state borrow ten generations foundation—house inherit hundred years enterprise—knights eat old virtue name clans—farmers serve ancestor fields furrow acres—merchants cultivate clan generations sell goods—artisans use great-great rules measures—bright ho hidden hidden—each obtain their place.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-three] Then capital faced capital and town touched town; houses rested on ten generations of merit and lands on a century of toil—scholars drew old patronymics, farmers worked ancestral plots, merchants traded crafts handed down, artisans followed ancient gauges—and under that steady glow each estate kept its station.

sentences.zh: [二四] 注[一]大武謂大陳武事也。

translations.literal: [Twenty-four] Comment [one] Great Martial means great array martial affairs.
translations.idiomatic: [Twenty-four] Comment [one]: "Great Martial" names the grand martial review.

sentences.zh: 月令「孟冬之月，天子乃命將帥講武，習射御」也。

translations.literal: Monthly Ordinances "First Winter month—Son of Heaven orders generals lecture martial—practice archery driving".
translations.idiomatic: The *Monthly Ordinances* says that in the first month of winter the emperor orders commanders to drill troops and practice archery and charioteering.

sentences.zh: 注[二]荊州，江、湘之地，其俗習於捕鳥，故使起之。

translations.literal: Comment [two] Jingzhou—Yangzi, Xiang lands—its customs practiced capture birds—therefore dispatch raise.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [two]: Jingzhou along Yangzi and Xiang bred bird catchers, hence they were ordered to flush fowl.

sentences.zh: 梁野，巴、漢之人，其俗習於逐獸，故使其人驅之。

translations.literal: Liang wilds—Ba, Han people—its customs practiced chase beasts—therefore make its people drive.
translations.idiomatic: The Liang wilderness drew Ba and Han folk skilled at driving game, so they were charged with stampeding quarry.

sentences.zh: 闐音田。

translations.literal: Tian sound tian (field).
translations.idiomatic: *Tian* is read *tian* (field).

sentences.zh: 聚音才諭反。

translations.literal: Ju sound cai yu fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Ju* follows the *cai yu* fanqie.

sentences.zh: 注[三]前書曰：「上林苑屬水衡都尉。

translations.literal: Comment [three] Former Documents says: "Upper Grove Park belongs Water Superintendent Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [three]: The *Former Han History* places Upper Grove Park under the Water Superintendent.

sentences.zh: 虞人，掌山澤之官。

translations.literal: Yu ren—manage mountains marshes officers.
translations.idiomatic: Foresters were officers charged with hills and wetlands.

sentences.zh: 」周禮曰：「虞人萊所田之野為表。

translations.literal: " Zhou Rites says: "Forester weed place hunt field make marker.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Zhou Rites* says foresters clear the hunting ground and set line markers."

sentences.zh: 」鄭司農曰：「表，所以識正行列也。

translations.literal: " Zheng Sinong says: "Biao—used mark align ranks lines.
translations.idiomatic: " Zheng Sinong explains that markers keep the lines straight."

sentences.zh: 」續漢書「將軍領軍皆有部，大將軍營五部，部校尉一人，部下有曲，曲有軍候一人」也。

translations.literal: " Continued Han History "generals lead armies all have divisions—great general camp five divisions—division colonel one man—division below have companies—company have army warden one man" also.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Continued Han History* states that every command has companies, a great general has five, each with a colonel, each company with a warden."

sentences.zh: 注[四]鄭玄注禮記曰：「獸□曰罘。

translations.literal: Comment [four] Zheng Xuan notes Record of Rites says: "Beast [lacuna] called fu.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [four]: Zheng Xuan's *Record of Rites* glosses the beast-net as *fu*.

sentences.zh: 」音浮。

translations.literal: " sound fu.
translations.idiomatic: " Read *fu* (float)."

sentences.zh: 紘，罘之綱。

translations.literal: Hong—fu's cord.
translations.idiomatic: *Hong* is the main rope of the mesh net.

sentences.zh: 注[五]蔡邕獨斷曰：「天子至尊，不敢渫瀆言之，故托於乘輿。

translations.literal: Comment [five] Cai Yong Sole Judgment says: "Son of Heaven utmost revered—not dare lightly pollute speak—therefore entrust ride carriage.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [five]: Cai Yong's *Sole Judgments* says subjects avoid blunt references to the emperor by speaking of his carriage instead.

sentences.zh: 天子車駕有大駕、法駕、小駕。

translations.literal: Son of Heaven chariot escort has grand escort—law escort—small escort.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial processions were graded grand, formal, or minor.

sentences.zh: 大駕則公卿奉引，備千乘萬騎。

translations.literal: Grand escort then dukes ministers guide lead—prepare thousand teams myriad riders.
translations.idiomatic: The grand escort paired dukes and ministers as outriders with thousands of chariots and riders.

sentences.zh: 法駕，公*[卿]*不在鹵簿中，唯執金吾奉引，侍中驂乘。

translations.literal: Law escort—dukes *[ministers]* not in roster—only Bearer of the Mace guides—gentleman attendant reins flank.
translations.idiomatic: For formal escort the Three Lords stood outside the procession roster; only the Bearer of the Mace led and the attendant rode beside.

sentences.zh: 」飛廉，館名也，武帝所作。

translations.literal: " Flying Gale—lodge name—Emperor Wu made.
translations.idiomatic: " Flying Gale was a lodge complex built by Emperor Wu."

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「飛廉，神禽，能致風氣，身似鹿，頭如雀，有角而蛇尾，文如豹文。

translations.literal: Former Documents Sounds Meanings says: "Flying Gale—divine bird—can summon wind qi—body like deer—head like sparrow—have horn yet snake tail—pattern like leopard pattern.
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han Sounds and Meanings* describes Flying Gale as a wind-summoning spirit bird—deer-like body, sparrow head, horned, snake tail, leopard markings.

sentences.zh: 於館上作之，因以名焉。」

translations.literal: At lodge above made it—thereby name it."
translations.idiomatic: They molded the image atop the lodge and named it accordingly."

sentences.zh: 注[六]酆，文王所都，在鄠縣東。

translations.literal: Comment [six] Feng—King Wen's capital—at Hu county east.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [six]: Feng was King Wen's seat east of Hu county.

sentences.zh: 鎬，武王所都，在上林苑中。

translations.literal: Hao—King Wu's capital—inside Upper Grove Park.
translations.idiomatic: Hao was King Wu's capital within Upper Grove Park.

sentences.zh: 三輔黃圖雲，上林苑有上蘭觀。

translations.literal: Three Adjuncts Yellow Chart says—Upper Grove Park has Upper Orchid Terrace.
translations.idiomatic: The *Yellow Chart of the Three Adjuncts* lists Upper Orchid Terrace in Upper Grove.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「司馬掌邦政，統六師。

translations.literal: Shang Documents says: "Minister of War grasps state policy—command six hosts.
translations.idiomatic: The *Scriptures* charges the Minister of War with the six hosts.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「百獸率舞。

translations.literal: " Also says: "Hundred beasts lead dance.
translations.idiomatic: " It also says the beasts dance in step."

sentences.zh: 」駭殫，言驚懼也。

translations.literal: " Hai dan—say alarm fear.
translations.idiomatic: " *Hai dan* stresses panic and exhaustion."

sentences.zh: 震震爚爚，奔走之貌。

translations.literal: Quake quake blaze blaze—run rush appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Quaking and flashing describes the stampede.

sentences.zh: 爚音躍。

translations.literal: Yue sound yue (leap).
translations.idiomatic: *Yue* is read like *yue* (leap).

sentences.zh: 塗，□也。

translations.literal: Tu—[lacuna].
translations.idiomatic: *Tu* means smeared flat across the ground.

sentences.zh: 反覆猶傾動也。

translations.literal: Overturn still tilt shake.
translations.idiomatic: "Overturn" here means tottering as if the hills swayed.

sentences.zh: 車騎既多，視之眩亂，有似傾動。

translations.literal: Chariots riders already many—view dizzy chaos—seem tilt shake.
translations.idiomatic: So many horsemen blurred the sight until hills seemed to reel.

sentences.zh: 蹂，踐也，音汝九反。

translations.literal: Rou—trample—sound ru jiu fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Rou* means to trample; follow the *ru jiu* fanqie.

sentences.zh: 蹸，轢也，音力刃反。

translations.literal: Lin—crush—sound li ren fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Lin* means to crush under wheels; follow *li ren*.

sentences.zh: 拗猶抑也，音於六反。

translations.literal: Ao still suppress—sound yu liu fan.
translations.idiomatic: *Ao* means to restrain; read with *yu liu*.

sentences.zh: 言且抑六師之怒而少停也。

translations.literal: Say temporarily suppress six hosts' wrath yet slight halt.
translations.idiomatic: This describes briefly checking the hosts' fury.

sentences.zh: 注[七]前書曰，武帝與北地良家子期於殿門，故號「期門」。

translations.literal: Comment [seven] Former Documents says—Emperor Wu with Beidi good-house sons pledge at hall gate—thus title "Gate Terminus".
translations.idiomatic: Comment [seven]: The *Former Han History* says Emperor Wu drilled Beidi yeomen at the palace gate—hence "Gate Guards."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「募佽飛射士」。

translations.literal: Also says: "Recruit Ci-Fei archers."
translations.idiomatic: It also mentions recruiting Ci-Fei bowmen.

sentences.zh: 音義：「佽飛，本秦左弋官也。

translations.literal: Sounds Meanings: "Ci-Fei—originally Qin Left Archer office.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss traces Ci-Fei to Qin's Left Archer bureau.

sentences.zh: 武帝改為佽飛官，有一令九丞，在上林中。

translations.literal: Emperor Wu changed to Ci-Fei office—have one director nine assistants—inside Upper Grove.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu renamed it the Ci-Fei office—one director and nine assistants inside Upper Grove.

sentences.zh: 紡矰繳，弋鳧鴈，歲萬頭，以供宗廟。

translations.literal: Spin bird bolts—shoot ducks wild geese—year myriad heads—to supply ancestral temples.
translations.idiomatic: They spun cords for fowling bolts, shot ducks and geese—tens of thousands yearly—for ancestral offerings.

sentences.zh: 」蒼頡篇曰：「攢，聚也。

translations.literal: " Cangjie Primer says: "Cuan—gather.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Cangjie Primer* glosses *cuan* as "mass together."

sentences.zh: 」「鑽」與「攢」通。

translations.literal: " "zuan" connects with "cuan".
translations.idiomatic: " The graphs *zuan* and *cuan* were interchangeable."

sentences.zh: 爾雅曰：「金鏃翦羽謂之鍭。

translations.literal: Erya says: "Metal point shear feather called hou.
translations.idiomatic: The *Erya* defines metal broadheads as *hou*.

sentences.zh: 」音侯。

translations.literal: " sound hou.
translations.idiomatic: " Read *hou*."

sentences.zh: 廣雅曰：「趹，奔也。

translations.literal: Guangya says: "Gui—run.
translations.idiomatic: The *Guangya* glosses *gui* as bolting.

sentences.zh: 」音決。

translations.literal: " sound jue.
translations.idiomatic: " Read *jue* (decide)."

sentences.zh: 機，弩牙也。

translations.literal: Ji—crossbow tooth.
translations.idiomatic: *Ji* is the crossbow catch.

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「掎，偏引也。

translations.literal: Shuowen says: "Ji—side pull.
translations.idiomatic: The *Shuowen* defines *ji* as hauling sideways.

sentences.zh: 」音居綺反。

translations.literal: " sound ju qi fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Follow the *ju qi* fanqie."

sentences.zh: 颮颮紛紛，觿多也。

translations.literal: Whirl whirl riot riot—horn knives many.
translations.idiomatic: Whirling chaos means sheer multitude.

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「颮，古坎字。

translations.literal: Shuowen says: "Biao—ancient kan graph.
translations.idiomatic: The *Shuowen* treats *biao* as an old form of *kan*.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注周禮曰：「結繳於矢謂之矰。

translations.literal: " Zheng Xuan notes Zhou Rites says: "Tie cord on arrow called zeng.
translations.idiomatic: " Zheng Xuan's *Zhou Rites* note calls corded arrows *zeng*."

sentences.zh: 」矰，高也。

translations.literal: " zeng—high.
translations.idiomatic: " *Zeng* also implies lofted shots."

sentences.zh: 注[八]郭璞注山海經曰：「譸似猴而大，臂長，便捷，色黑。

translations.literal: Comment [eight] Guo Pu notes Classic of Mountains and Seas says: "Zhou like monkey yet large—arms long—nimble—color black.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [eight]: Guo Pu's *Classic of Mountains and Seas* calls *zhou* a large black ape with long arms.

sentences.zh: 」蒼頡書曰：「狖似狸。

translations.literal: " Cangjie Book says: "You like raccoon dog.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Cangjie Book* compares *you* to a civet."

sentences.zh: 」音以救反。

translations.literal: " sound yi jiu fan.
translations.idiomatic: " Follow *yi jiu*."

sentences.zh: 淮南子曰：「譸狖顛□而失木枝。

translations.literal: Huainanzi says: "Zhou you topple [lacuna] lose wood branches.

translations.literal: '" Say today match Yao.
translations.idiomatic: " This asserts that the present reign rivals Yao's peace."

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「宣帝頗好神仙，王□、張子僑等並待詔，所幸宮館，輒為歌頌，第其高下，以差賜帛焉。」

translations.literal: Former Documents says: "Emperor Xuan rather fond transcendents—Wang [lacuna], Zhang Ziqiao etc. await edicts—favored palace lodges—then compose hymns—rank their high low—by grades grant silk."
translations.idiomatic: The *Former Han History* says Emperor Xuan favored immortality seekers; Wang [lacuna], Zhang Ziqiao, and others sang hymns at favored lodges and received silk graded by merit.

sentences.zh: 注[二四]十代、百年，並舉全數也。

translations.literal: Comment [twenty-four] Ten generations, hundred years—both raise whole counts.
translations.idiomatic: Comment [twenty-four]: "Ten generations" and "a century" state full tallies.

sentences.zh: 易曰：「食舊德，貞厲終吉。

translations.literal: Changes says: "Eat old virtue—divination stern end auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: The *Changes* speaks of feeding on inherited virtue—though stern, ending auspiciously.

sentences.zh: 」谷梁傳曰：「古者有士人、商人、農人、工人。

translations.literal: " Guliang Tradition says: "Ancient have knight men, merchant men, farmer men, artisan men.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Guliang Tradition* lists knights, merchants, farmers, and artisans of antiquity."

sentences.zh: 」淮南子曰「古者至德之時，賈便其肆，農安其業，大夫安其職，而處士修其道」也。

translations.literal: " Huainanzi says "Ancient utmost virtue times—merchant ease their stalls—farmer rest their tasks—great officers rest their posts—retired scholars cultivate their way" also.
translations.idiomatic: " The *Huainanzi* praises an age when merchants, farmers, officers, and recluses each kept their proper sphere."

sentences.zh: 若臣者，徒觀多乎舊墟，聞之乎故老，什分而未得其一端，故不能□舉也。

translations.literal: Like your servant—only observe much at old ruins—hear from old elders—ten parts yet obtain not one end—therefore cannot [lacuna] raise.
translations.idiomatic: As for your servant, I have toured ruins and questioned elders yet grasp only a tithe—too little to cite fully.

sentences.zh: 校勘記

translations.literal: Collation notes (section heading).
translations.idiomatic: Editorial collation notes (section title).

sentences.zh: 一三二三頁四行今洺州永*

translations.literal: Page 1323 line 4—now Mingzhou Yong *
translations.idiomatic: Page 1323, line 4 begins “now Mingzhou Yongping county”; the line continues after the gloss.

translations.literal: (ping)
translations.idiomatic: (The gloss supplies the graph ping, completing the placename.)

sentences.zh: **[年]*縣也集解引沉欽韓說，謂「永平縣」當作「永年縣」，今據改。

translations.literal: **[year]* county—Collected Commentaries cite Shen Qinhan—say "Yongping county" ought write "Yongnian county"—now according amend.
translations.idiomatic: The sentence identifies Yongping county. The *Collected Commentaries* cite Shen Qinhan: the name should read Yongnian county, and the text is emended accordingly.

sentences.zh: 按：「洺」原作「洛」，形近而鬥，徑據殿本改正。

translations.literal: Note: "Ming" originally "Luo"—forms near confuse—directly according palace edition correct.
translations.idiomatic: Note: "Ming" was miswritten "Luo" from graphic similarity; it is corrected from the palace edition.

sentences.zh: 一三二三頁八行漢承秦制改立郡縣按：張森楷校勘記謂「改」當依前書作「並」，既承秦制，則非漢所改也。

translations.literal: Page 1323 line 8—Han inherit Qin system alter establish commanderies counties—Note: Zhang Senkai collation says "alter" ought follow Former Documents write "together"—since inherit Qin system—not Han altered.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1323, line 8: Zhang Senkai's collation argues "alter" should match the *Former Han History* as "together"; Han inherited Qin's system rather than replacing it wholesale.

sentences.zh: 一三二四頁一行劉季逐而羈之按：集解引王補說，謂「羈」前書□傳作「掎」，通鑒亦作「掎」，用左傳「晉人角之，諸戎掎之。」

translations.literal: Page 1324 line 1—Liu Ji pursue restrain—Note: Collected Commentaries cite Wang Bu—say "restrain" Former Documents [lacuna] biography write "drag flank"—Comprehensive Mirror also write "drag flank"—use Zuo Tradition "Jin men horn—various Rong drag flank."
translations.idiomatic: Page 1324, line 1: Wang Bu notes "restrain" matches "drag flank" in the *Former Han* biography and *Zizhi Tongjian*, echoing the *Zuo Tradition*.

sentences.zh: 一三二六頁三行見趙岐孟子注「岐」原斗「歧」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1326 line 3—see Zhao Qi Mencius note "Qi" originally fault "branch"—directly correct.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1326, line 3: Zhao Qi's Mencius commentary had "Qi" misprinted as "branch"; corrected directly.

sentences.zh: 按：紹興本趙岐之「岐」皆斗「歧」，後如此，不悉出校記。

translations.literal: Note: Shaoxing edition Zhao Qi "Qi" all fault "branch"—later thus—not all emerge collation notes.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Shaoxing editions misprint Zhao Qi's "Qi" throughout; later instances are silently corrected without repeating notes.

sentences.zh: 一三二六頁一四行豈若卑論齊俗按：「齊」當依史記作「儕」。

translations.literal: Page 1326 line 14—how like humble discuss Qi customs—Note: "Qi" ought follow Records as "peer".
translations.idiomatic: Page 1326, line 14: "Qi" should follow the *Records* as "peer among equals," not "Qi customs."

sentences.zh: 一三二七頁一一行彪復辟司徒玉況府汲本、殿本「玉」作「玊」。

translations.literal: Page 1327 line 11—Biao restore minister education Yu Kuang mansion—Ji edition, palace edition "Yu" write variant jade.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1327, line 11: Ji and palace editions write Yu Kuang's surname with a variant graph.

sentences.zh: 按：玉字本有肅音，不必改為「玊」，參閱前虞延傳校記。

translations.literal: Note: Yu graph originally has su sound—not need change to variant—refer earlier Yu Yan biography collation.
translations.idiomatic: Note: "Yu" already carries the proper reading; no need for the variant—see the collation on Yu Yan's biography.

sentences.zh: 又按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂是時「司徒」上有「大」字。

translations.literal: Also note: Collected Commentaries cite Shen Qinhan—say this time "minister education" above has "grand" graph.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Qinhan adds that "Grand Minister of Education" should carry the prefix "grand."

sentences.zh: 一三二八頁一行不能無*[為]*惡據集解本補。

translations.literal: Page 1328 line 1—cannot without *[do]* evil—according Collected Commentaries supplement.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1328, line 1: "Cannot fail to do evil"—supplied from the *Collected Commentaries*.

sentences.zh: 按：此所引賈誼上疏之辭與前書不同，前書作「習與正人居之，不能毋正，猶生長於齊，不能不齊言也。

translations.literal: Note: This cites Jia Yi memorial wording differs Former Documents—Former Documents writes "Practice with upright men dwell—not fail upright—like grow at Qi—not fail Qi speech.
translations.idiomatic: Note: This differs from the *Former Han History*, which reads that habit with the upright keeps one upright, as growing up in Qi teaches Qi speech.

sentences.zh: 習與不正人居也，不能毋不正，猶生長於楚之地，不能不楚言也」。

translations.literal: Practice with not upright men dwell—not fail not upright—like grow Chu lands—not fail Chu speech".
translations.idiomatic: Habit with the crooked bends one crooked, as growing in Chu teaches Chu speech."

sentences.zh: 一三二八頁二行出則周公邵公太*

translations.literal: Page 1328 line 2—go out then Duke Zhou Duke Shao Grand *
translations.idiomatic: Page 1328, line 2 lists figures who attended the ruler abroad: Duke Zhou, Duke Shao, and the Grand Duke (the line breaks before the final word, supplied by the gloss).

translations.literal: (duke)
translations.idiomatic: (The gloss completes "Grand Duke.")

sentences.zh: *史佚據汲本刪。

translations.literal: * Historian Yi—according Ji edition delete.
translations.idiomatic: The phrase naming Yi the Historian is deleted per the Ji manuscript.

sentences.zh: 按：史記云「召公為師，周公為保」，無太公輔成王事，「公」字衍。

translations.literal: Note: Records says "Duke Shao tutor—Duke Zhou protector"—no Grand Duke aid Cheng king affair—"duke" graph redundant.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The *Records* pairs Duke Shao as tutor and Duke Zhou as protector—no Grand Duke assisting Cheng; the extra "duke" is spurious.

sentences.zh: 太史佚即史佚也。

translations.literal: Grand Historian Yi namely Historian Yi.
translations.idiomatic: "Grand Historian Yi" is simply Yi the Historian.

sentences.zh: 一三二八頁一五行使僕中允按：沉家本謂「允」續志作「盾」。

translations.literal: Page 1328 line 15—envoy steward middle yun—Note: Shen Jiaben says "yun" Continued Treatise writes "shield".
translations.idiomatic: Page 1328, line 15: Shen Jiaben notes "yun" appears as "shield" in the *Continued Treatise*.

sentences.zh: 一三二九頁四行故令誼傅之按：「令」原斗「今」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1329 line 4—therefore order Yi tutor—Note: "order" originally fault "now"—directly correct.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1329, line 4: "Order" was misprinted "now"; corrected.

sentences.zh: 一三三０頁五行誦詩賦按：汲本「賦」作「書」。

translations.literal: Page 1330 line 5—chant poetry rhapsody—Note: Ji edition "rhapsody" write "document".
translations.idiomatic: Page 1330, line 5: The Ji edition reads "documents" instead of "rhapsodies."

sentences.zh: 一三三一頁一二行負薪之諾按：汲本、殿本「諾」作「語」。

translations.literal: Page 1331 line 12—carry firewood promise—Note: Ji edition, palace edition "promise" write "speech".
translations.idiomatic: Page 1331, line 12: Ji and palace editions read "speech" for "promise."

sentences.zh: 一三三二頁一三行執文之德按：集解引周壽昌說，謂周頌作「秉文之德」，此「秉」字作「執」，乃唐諱□，秉與□同音，嫌名也，故避「秉」為「執」，義同字異。

translations.literal: Page 1332 line 13—grasp pattern virtue—Note: Collected Commentaries cite Zhou Shouchang—say Zhou Hymns write "hold pattern virtue"—this "hold" graph write "grasp"—is Tang taboo [lacuna]—hold same sound taboo—disliked name—therefore avoid "hold" as "grasp"—meaning same graph different.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1332, line 13: Zhou Shouchang notes the *Zhou Hymns* read "hold cultivated virtue"; Tang taboo replaced "hold" with "grasp"—same sense, alternate graph.

sentences.zh: 一三三三頁二行舟人吉桑對曰按：「吉桑」新序作「固桑」，說苑尊賢篇作「古乘」，人表作「固來」，循吏傳注作「古桑」。

translations.literal: Page 1333 line 2—boatman Ji Sang reply—Note: "Ji Sang" New Preface write "Gu Sang"—Garden of Stories Esteem Worthies writes "Gu Cheng"—Tables writes "Gu Lai"—Honest Officials biography note writes "Gu Sang".
translations.idiomatic: Page 1333, line 2: Parallel texts vary "Ji Sang" as Gu Sang, Gu Cheng, Gu Lai, or Gu Sang across *New Preface*, *Garden of Stories*, and commentaries.

sentences.zh: 沉欽韓謂「乘」「來」皆「桑」之誤，「吉」又為「古」之誤。

translations.literal: Shen Qinhan says "cheng" "lai" all "mulberry" fault—"auspicious" again "ancient" fault.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Qinhan argues "cheng" and "lai" corrupt "sang," and "ji" corrupts "gu."

sentences.zh: 一三三四頁三行召詣校書部按：「校書部」疑當作「校書郎」。

translations.literal: Page 1334 line 3—summoned to Collate Books Bureau—Note: "Collate Books Bureau" should probably read "collating secretary."
translations.idiomatic: Page 1334, line 3: the gloss naming the collating-books bureau should read collating secretary.

sentences.zh: 御覽五一五引正作「校書郎」，又班超傳云「兄固，被召詣校書郎」。

translations.literal: Imperial Digest 515 cites correctly "collating secretary"—also Ban Chao biography says "brother Gu, summoned to collating secretary."
translations.idiomatic: The *Imperial Digest* reads correctly, matching Ban Gu's summons in Ban Chao's biography.

sentences.zh: 一三三四頁四行司隸從事孟異按：集解引惠棟說，謂「異」當作「冀」，見馬援、杜林等傳。

translations.literal: Page 1334 line 4—Regional Inspector aide Meng Yi—Note: *Collected Commentaries* cites Hui Dong—say "Yi" should write "Ji"—see Ma Yuan, Du Lin biographies.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1334, line 4: Hui Dong argues the aide's name should be Meng Ji, per parallel passages.

sentences.zh: 又引沉欽韓說，謂史通正史篇作「孟冀」。

translations.literal: Also cites Shen Qinhan—say *Comprehensive Histories* Orthodox Histories chapter writes "Meng Ji."
translations.idiomatic: Shen Qinhan notes *Shitong* agrees on Meng Ji.

sentences.zh: 一三三四頁一三行六代謂武帝史臣謂司馬遷也按：此注原誤置於「史臣」之下，今移正。

translations.literal: Page 1334 line 13—"six generations" means Emperor Wu—"court historian" means Sima Qian—Note: this comment was misplaced below "court historian"; now moved.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1334, line 13: the comment was repositioned because "six generations" ends one clause and "court historian" opens the next.

sentences.zh: 蓋正文「六世」句絕，「史臣」屬下為句，若注於「史臣」之下，則「史臣」二字當連「六世」為句矣。

translations.literal: Because main text "six generations" sentence breaks—"court historian" attaches below—if comment follows "court historian"—then "court historian" wrongly joins "six generations."
translations.idiomatic: The note clarifies punctuation: "six generations" must stand alone before "the court historian Sima Qian."

sentences.zh: 一三三五頁一一行劉敬說上都關中殿本「劉」作「婁」。

translations.literal: Page 1335 line 11—Liu Jing urges capital in Guanzhong—palace edition "Liu" writes "Lou."
translations.idiomatic: Page 1335, line 11: some editions write Lou Jing instead of Liu Jing.

sentences.zh: 按：婁敬說高祖都關中，封奉春君，賜姓劉氏，故亦作「劉敬」，然下文「奉春建策」注又作「婁敬」，前後亦不一致也。

translations.literal: Note: Lou Jing persuaded Gaozu to settle Guanzhong—was ennobled Lord of Fengchun—given surname Liu—thus also "Liu Jing"—yet later "Fengchun planned strategy" note again writes "Lou Jing"—front back inconsistent.
translations.idiomatic: Lou Jing became Liu Jing after receiving the imperial surname; editions vary between the two forms.

sentences.zh: 一三三五頁一五行表以*

translations.literal: Page 1335 line 15—chart using *
translations.idiomatic: Page 1335, line 15: the phrase "chart using" breaks before the gloss.

translations.literal: (Tai)
translations.idiomatic: (The gloss supplies tai for the sacred peak graph.)

sentences.zh: **[太]*華終南之山張森楷校勘記謂「太華」字本不作「泰」，後人誤以為范曄避其父諱，改「泰」為「太」，遂並非諱改者而亦回改為「泰」。

translations.literal: **[Tai]*hua Zhongnan mountains—Zhang Senkai collation says "Taihua" originally was not written "Tai" with water radical—later readers wrongly thought Fan Ye avoided his father's taboo—changed tai to tai—then wrongly restored graphs that were never tabooed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Senkai explains emendation between Taihua and graph variants for Mount Hua.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now according amend.
translations.idiomatic: The text is revised accordingly.

sentences.zh: 一三三五頁一六行帶以洪河涇渭之川按：校補謂文選此下有「觿流之隈，汧湧其西」語。

translations.literal: Page 1335 line 16—gird with Hong Yellow River Jing Wei rivers—Note: collation supplement says *Wen xuan* adds "eddies coil west" lines.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1335, line 16: *Wen xuan* preserves longer wording about western rivers.

sentences.zh: 一三三六頁四行度宏規而大起惠棟謂李善曰「度」或為「慶」，慶與羌古字通，小爾雅雲羌，發聲也。

translations.literal: Page 1336 line 4—measure grand plan then huge rise—Hui Dong says Li Shan reads "measure" or "celebrate"—celebrate connects with qiang ancient graph—Minor Erya says qiang is sentence opener.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1336, line 4: textual debate over measure versus exclamatory "qing."

sentences.zh: 按：王念孫謂李善本度字本作「慶」，今本作「度」者，後人據五臣本及班固傳改之耳。

translations.literal: Note: Wang Niansun says Li Shan edition originally wrote "celebrate"—present "measure" comes from later editors following Five Ministers edition and Ban Gu biography.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Niansun traces corruption from Li Shan to Five Ministers recensions.

sentences.zh: 善注原文當云「小雅曰羌，發聲也，『慶』與『羌』古字通，『慶』或為『庋』。

translations.literal: Shan commentary original ought read "Minor Odes says qiang opens sentences—celebrate links qiang ancient graph—celebrate sometimes written gui.
translations.idiomatic: Reconstructed Li Shan wording on qiang and celebrate.

sentences.zh: 」又謂作「慶」是。

translations.literal: " Also says writing "celebrate" correct.
translations.idiomatic: He affirms the "celebrate" reading.

sentences.zh: 慶，語詞。

translations.literal: Celebrate—function word.
translations.idiomatic: Here it is a particle, not "celebrate" in praise.

sentences.zh: 「宏規」與「大起」相對為文，言肇建都邑，先宏規之而後大起之也。

translations.literal: "Grand plan" pairs "huge rise"—means founding capital—first plan then massive build.
translations.idiomatic: Parallel couplet: plan first, erect afterward.

sentences.zh: 一三三六頁五行故窮奢而極侈按：王先謙謂固集及文選「奢」並作「泰」，此亦范氏避其父諱而改。

translations.literal: Page 1336 line 5—thus utmost luxury utmost extravagance—Note: Wang Xianqian says Ban corpora and *Wen xuan* write utmost ease for tai—Fan altered for father's taboo.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1336, line 5: Fan Ye avoided "tai" by writing "luxury" where Ban used tai.

sentences.zh: 一三三六頁八行鄉曲豪俊遊俠之雄按：文選「俊」作「舉」，李注引史記魏公子無忌曰「平原之遊，徒豪舉耳」。

translations.literal: Page 1336 line 8—hamlet heroes wandering champions—Note: *Wen xuan* "hero" writes "swagger"—Li cites Prince Xinping calling Pingyuan swagger.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1336, line 8: *Wen xuan* reads village swagger versus local heroes.

sentences.zh: 蓋以「鄉曲豪舉」為句。

translations.literal: Probably take "hamlet swagger" as phrase.
translations.idiomatic: One punctuation treats swagger as object.

sentences.zh: 此以「鄉曲豪俊」與「遊俠之雄」連讀為句，故注云「豪俊遊俠謂朱家、郭解、原涉之類也」。

translations.literal: This joins "hamlet heroes" with "champions"—hence comment names Zhu Jia, Guo Jie types.
translations.idiomatic: Another reading pairs heroes with wandering bravos.

sentences.zh: 一三三七頁一二行天子城十二門通十二子按：此周禮「匠人營國方九里旁三門」鄭玄注文，章懷引之以釋「立十二之通門」也。

translations.literal: Page 1337 line 12—Son of Heaven wall twelve gates match twelve branches—Note: this is Zheng Xuan gloss on Zhouli craftsman nine li three gates—Prince Zhang cites it for "twelve thorough gates."
translations.idiomatic: Page 1337, line 12: Li Xian borrowed Zheng Xuan's twelve-gate scheme.

sentences.zh: 文選注同。

translations.literal: *Wen xuan* commentary agrees.
translations.idiomatic: The *Wen xuan* note matches.

sentences.zh: 各本誤引周禮地官「司門」鄭注，作「司門若今城門校尉，主王城十二門」。

translations.literal: Various editions wrongly cite Zhouli Earth Offices Gate Keeper Zheng note—as Gate Keeper like gate colonel—guarding king wall twelve gates.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier editions misattribute the gloss to Gate Keeper chapter.

sentences.zh: 一三三七頁一三行漢宮閣疏曰汲本、殿本「閣」作「闕」。

translations.literal: Page 1337 line 13—Han Palace Pavilion Digest says—Ji edition palace edition "pavilion" writes "tower."
translations.idiomatic: Page 1337, line 13: manuscript variants between pavilion and tower graphs.

sentences.zh: 按：後文「披香」注引「漢宮閣名」，殿本「閣」作「闕」，文選注亦作「闕」。

translations.literal: Note: later "Fragrant Drape" note cites "Han palace pavilion names"—palace edition "pavilion" writes "tower"—*Wen xuan* note also writes "tower."
translations.idiomatic: Parallel passages prefer the tower graph.

sentences.zh: 又後文「左牽牛而右織女」注引「漢宮閣疏」，殿本亦作「閣」，而文選注則作「闕」。

translations.literal: Again later "Oxherd Weaver" note cites "Han Palace Pavilion Digest"—palace edition still "pavilion"—yet *Wen xuan* note writes "tower."
translations.idiomatic: Mixed usage across citations.

sentences.zh: 又按：「漢宮閣疏」或「漢宮闕疏」與「漢宮閣名」或「漢宮闕名」，隋志俱不著錄，唐志有漢宮闕簿，史記高祖紀索隱、初學記居處部、御覽居處部十二引「漢宮殿疏」，北堂書鈔舟部上引「漢宮室疏」，殆即一書也。

translations.literal: Again note: Han palace pavilion digest versus tower digest versus pavilion names versus tower names—not listed Sui catalog—Tang catalog has Han palace tower register—*Records* Gaozu Suoyin, *Chuxue ji*, *Imperial Digest* cite Han palace hall digest—Northern Hall transcripts boats cite Han chamber digest—probably same book.
translations.idiomatic: Bibliographic essay tracing digest titles to one lost handbook.

sentences.zh: 一三三八頁一行原嘗*[謂]*平原君趙勝孟嘗君田文也據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Page 1338 line 1—Lord Ping Lord Chang *[meaning]* Lord Ping Zhao Sheng Lord Chang Tian Wen—according Ji palace supplement.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1338, line 1: supplied gloss connecting Ping and Chang to the four lords.

sentences.zh: 一三三八頁五行逴犖諸夏按：李慈銘謂「犖」文選作「躒」。

translations.literal: Page 1338 line 5—stride towering Central States—Note: Li Ciming says "towering" *Wen xuan* writes "trample."
translations.idiomatic: Page 1338, line 5: variant graphs for striding over the realm.

sentences.zh: 一三三九頁一三行王□字子泉汲本、殿本「泉」作「淵」，下「泉雲頌歎」之「泉」亦作「淵」。

translations.literal: Page 1339 line 13—Wang [lacuna] courtesy Ziquan—Ji palace editions "quan" writes "yuan"—below "cloud hymn praise" quan also writes yuan.
translations.idiomatic: Page 1339, line 13: Yuan versus quan taboo variants.

sentences.zh: 按：「淵」作「泉」，當是章懷避唐諱改。

translations.literal: Note: yuan written quan—probably Prince Zhang avoiding Tang taboo.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian altered yuan to quan.
